811.description_en_long: An isolated 895 m peak in the southern Kitakami Highlands, in Ichinoseki, Iwate. Its gently trailing skirts earned it the name 'little Fuji,' and Sanriku fishermen long prayed here for safety at sea. In spring azaleas color the whole mountain.
811.description_en_short: An isolated peak called a 'little Fuji,' long revered by the fishermen of Sanriku.

812.description_ja_long: 新潟県柏崎市の山で、刈羽黒姫山とも呼ばれる刈羽三山の一峰。古くは機織りの娘たちが登拝した信仰の山で、頂近くの鵜川神社には鵜の群れが神を導いた伝説が残る。
812.description_ja_short: 機織りの娘たちが登拝した信仰の山。刈羽三山の一峰。
812.description_en_long: A mountain in Kashiwazaki, Niigata, one of the Kariwa Sanzan and also called Kariwa Kurohimesan. Long a mountain of faith climbed by young weaver women, it keeps a legend at Ukawa Shrine near the summit that a flock of cormorants guided the deity there.
812.description_en_short: A mountain of faith once climbed by weaver girls; one of the Kariwa Sanzan.

813.description_ja_long: 広島市北部、太田川と三篠川に抱かれてそびえる山塊の主峰。山を覆うササの葉が遠目に白く光り、雪のように見えたことが名の起こりと伝わる。頂は360度の展望を誇る。
813.description_ja_short: ササの葉が白く光る姿が名の由来という広島市北部の主峰。
813.description_en_long: Chief peak of the massif embraced by the Ota and Misasa rivers in northern Hiroshima City. Its name is said to come from the sasa bamboo covering the mountain, whose leaves gleamed white from afar like snow. The summit boasts a 360-degree panorama.
813.description_en_short: A northern Hiroshima peak said to be named for sasa leaves that shone white like snow.

814.description_ja_long: 静岡県西部、赤石山脈の南端に連なる火防信仰の霊山。山上の秋葉神社と秋葉寺は全国から信者を集め、各地に残る秋葉街道の名はその名残。老杉の森が頂を包む。
814.description_ja_short: 火防の神として知られる霊山。秋葉街道の名を各地に残す。
814.description_en_long: A sacred mountain of fire-prevention faith at the southern end of the Akaishi Range in western Shizuoka. Akiha Shrine and the temple Shuyoji on its heights drew believers nationwide, and the Akiha Kaido roads named for it remain across Japan. Old cedars wrap the summit.
814.description_en_short: A sacred mountain of the fire-preventing deity, its name carried by Akiha Kaido roads nationwide.

816.description_ja_long: 下北半島の最高峰で、恐山山地の南端にそびえる。頂には岳主神社の奥宮が祀られ信仰の山の面影を残す。眼下に陸奥湾とむつ市街を望み、夜景の美しさでも知られる。
816.description_ja_short: 下北半島の最高峰。陸奥湾を見下ろし、夜景も名高い。
816.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Shimokita Peninsula, rising at the southern end of the Osorezan mountains. The inner sanctuary of Gakushu Shrine on the summit recalls its past as a mountain of faith, and it overlooks Mutsu Bay and the city of Mutsu, famed too for night views.
816.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Shimokita Peninsula, overlooking Mutsu Bay and famed for its night views.

817.description_ja_long: 富士と並び称された東国の名山で、万葉集に25首も詠まれた歌の山。男体山と女体山の双耳峰にイザナギ・イザナミの二神を祀り、紫峰の雅称をもつ。標高877m。
817.description_ja_short: 富士と並び称され万葉集にも詠まれた東国の名山。双耳峰。
817.description_en_long: A famous mountain of eastern Japan once ranked with Mt. Fuji and sung in 25 poems of the Manyoshu. Its 877 m twin peaks, Nantaisan and Nyotaisan, enshrine the deities Izanagi and Izanami, and the mountain bears the elegant name Shiho, the 'purple peak.'
817.description_en_short: A twin-peaked mountain of eastern Japan, ranked with Mt. Fuji and sung in the Manyoshu.

818.description_ja_long: 埼玉県の比企三山に数えられる山で、頂に一等三角点と天測点を置く。かつて東京天文台の観測所が置かれた星の山として知られ、なだらかな頂は全方位の展望をもつ。
818.description_ja_short: かつて天文台の観測所が置かれた星の山。比企三山の一つ。
818.description_en_long: One of Saitama's Hiki Sanzan, its summit holding a first-order triangulation point and an astronomical azimuth point. Known as a 'mountain of stars' where a Tokyo Astronomical Observatory station once stood, its gentle top commands views in every direction.
818.description_en_short: A 'mountain of stars' that once hosted an observatory station; one of the Hiki Sanzan.

819.description_ja_long: 福島県の山。名は、矢を受けて逃げた暴れ猪を「きっとやこの辺に逃げた」と追った故事に由来すると伝わり、仕留めた矢は二ツ箭山に奉納されたという。標高875m。
819.description_ja_short: 猪を追う「きっとや」の言葉が名になったと伝わる山。
819.description_en_long: A mountain in Fukushima Prefecture. Its name is said to come from hunters chasing a raging boar that fled after taking an arrow, crying 'kitto ya' — 'it surely fled around here' — and the arrow that felled it was dedicated at Futatsuyasan. It rises 875 m.
819.description_en_short: A mountain said to take its name from 'kitto ya,' the cry of hunters chasing a boar.

820.description_ja_long: 岩手県、北上山地南部の信仰の山。野火がどれほどの大風でも大事に至らぬという故事から、火伏の「火の神」が氷上に転じた名と伝わる。頂は三陸の海を見晴らす。
820.description_ja_short: 火伏の「火の神」が名に転じたと伝わる三陸の信仰の山。
820.description_en_long: A mountain of faith in the southern Kitakami Highlands of Iwate. Its name is said to derive from a fire-quelling 'god of fire' (hi-no-kami) who kept wildfires in check however hard the wind blew, the word later shifting to Hikami. The summit overlooks the Sanriku sea.
820.description_en_short: A sacred Sanriku mountain whose name is said to come from a fire-quelling 'god of fire.'

821.description_ja_long: 北海道には天狗岳と呼ばれる山が24座もあるといわれ、その一つである当山は他と区別して余市天狗岳の名で呼ばれる。標高872m。
821.description_ja_short: 道内に数多い天狗岳の一つ。余市天狗岳の名で呼ばれる。
821.description_en_long: Hokkaido is said to have as many as 24 peaks named Tengudake, and this 872 m mountain is one of them, known as Yoichi Tengudake to distinguish it from all the others.
821.description_en_short: One of Hokkaido's many Tengudake peaks, distinguished by the name Yoichi Tengudake.

822.description_ja_long: 福井・京都の境に立つ丹波高地の山。遠くから望む姿が修験者の頭巾に似ることが名の由来とされ、頂には雨の神を祀る小さな祠が鎮まり、春の神事で賑わう。標高871m。
822.description_ja_short: 修験者の頭巾に似た姿が名の由来。頂に雨の神を祀る。
822.description_en_long: A peak of the Tanba Highlands on the border of Fukui and Kyoto. Seen from afar it resembles the hood (tokin) worn by mountain ascetics, said to be the origin of its name. A small shrine to a rain god rests quietly on the 871 m summit, which bustles during spring rites.
822.description_en_short: Named for its resemblance to a mountain ascetic's hood; a rain god is enshrined on its summit.

823.description_ja_long: 岩手県南部、種山高原の一等三角点峰。宮沢賢治の詩にも登場する詩情豊かな高原にあり、牛馬が草を食む牧歌的な風景の先に岩手山や早池峰山までも見渡す。
823.description_ja_short: 宮沢賢治ゆかりの種山高原の頂。牧歌的な風景が広がる。
823.description_en_long: A first-order triangulation peak on the Taneyama Highlands in southern Iwate, a lyrical plateau that appears in the poetry of Kenji Miyazawa. Beyond pastoral scenes of cattle and horses grazing, the view stretches as far as Mt. Iwate and Mt. Hayachine.
823.description_en_short: The top of the Taneyama Highlands, a pastoral landscape associated with poet Kenji Miyazawa.

824.description_ja_long: 和歌山県北部、高野山から西へ延びる長峰山脈の中ほどに立つ。山国和歌山では珍しい草原の頂をもち、秋には一面のススキの穂が波打つ。中央に笠石を据え、展望は360度。
824.description_ja_short: ススキの草原が頂に広がる和歌山県きっての高原の山。
824.description_en_long: Standing midway along the Nagamine Range that runs west from Koyasan in northern Wakayama. Its grassy summit, a rarity in this mountainous prefecture, ripples with waves of silver pampas grass in autumn. The Kasa-ishi rock sits at its center, with 360-degree views.
824.description_en_short: A summit spread with pampas-grass meadows, one of Wakayama's finest highland mountains.

826.description_ja_long: 北海道の赤平市と芦別市にまたがる音江連山の主峰で、標高864m。山頂に置かれた三等三角点は、山名と異なる「三又山」の名をもつ。
826.description_ja_short: 音江連山の主峰。山頂の三角点は「三又山」の名をもつ。
826.description_en_long: The main peak of the Otoe mountain range straddling the cities of Akabira and Ashibetsu in Hokkaido, standing 864 m. The third-order triangulation point placed on its summit bears a name different from the mountain's own: 'Mitsumatayama.'
826.description_en_short: Chief peak of the Otoe range; its summit triangulation point bears the name 'Mitsumatayama.'

827.description_ja_long: 島根県美郷町の山で、抜きん出た二つの主峰が冠のように見えることが名の由来。江戸期には不可志山の名で石見の名所に数えられた。頂の展望は360度に及ぶ。
827.description_ja_short: 二峰が冠に見えることが名の由来。石見の古い名所の山。
827.description_en_long: A mountain in the town of Misato, Shimane, named for its two prominent main peaks, which together look like a crown. In the Edo period it was counted among the famous sights of Iwami under the name Fukashiyama. The summit commands a full 360-degree panorama.
827.description_en_short: Named for twin peaks that resemble a crown; an old scenic landmark of the Iwami region.

828.description_ja_long: 岐阜県南西部、養老山地を代表する一等三角点の山で、旧名を多芸山という。麓には孝子伝説で名高い養老の滝がかかり、山中には原生林が静かに残る。標高859m。
828.description_ja_short: 麓に孝子伝説の養老の滝を抱く、養老山地を代表する山。
828.description_en_long: A first-order triangulation peak representing the Yoro Mountains in southwestern Gifu, formerly known as Tagiyama. At its foot hangs Yoro Falls, famed for a legend of filial piety, while virgin forest survives quietly on its slopes. Elevation 859 m.
828.description_en_short: The signature peak of the Yoro Mountains, with the legendary Yoro Falls at its foot.

829.description_ja_long: 大阪と和歌山の境に横たわる和泉山脈の主峰で、和泉葛城山と呼ばれる。頂には八大竜王を祀る社が府県それぞれに鎮まり、この緯度では珍しいブナの森が広がる。
829.description_ja_short: 頂に八大竜王を祀り、ブナの森が茂る和泉山脈の主峰。
829.description_en_long: The main peak of the Izumi Range lying along the Osaka-Wakayama border, known as Izumi Katsuragisan. Shrines to the Eight Great Dragon Kings stand on the summit, one on each prefecture's side, amid a beech forest rare at this latitude.
829.description_en_short: Chief peak of the Izumi Range, with dragon-king shrines on top amid a rare beech forest.

830.description_ja_long: 北海道東部、摩周湖の外輪山の最高峰。カムイヌプリ「神の山」とあがめられ、藻琴山らが投げた槍が頂に刺さったという伝説を残す火山。大きな火口を抱く。
830.description_ja_short: 「神の山」とあがめられてきた摩周湖の外輪山の最高峰。
830.description_en_long: The highest peak on the caldera rim of Lake Mashu in eastern Hokkaido. Revered as Kamuinupuri, 'mountain of the gods,' this volcano cradles a large crater and keeps a legend that a spear hurled by Mokotoyama and others pierced its summit.
830.description_en_short: The highest peak on Lake Mashu's caldera rim, long revered as the 'mountain of the gods.'

831.description_ja_long: 高知県四万十市と四万十町にまたがる四国西南山地の山。室町期、一条教房が街道整備の折に地蔵を祀るお堂を建てたことが山名の由来と伝わる。標高857m。
831.description_ja_short: 山頂に古いお堂が立つ四国西南山地の山。名は室町期の地蔵堂に由来。
831.description_en_long: A mountain of the southwestern Shikoku ranges straddling Shimanto City and Shimanto Town in Kochi. The name is said to derive from a hall enshrining Jizo that Ichijo Norifusa built in the Muromachi period while improving the highway. Elevation 857 m.
831.description_en_short: A southwestern Shikoku peak topped by an old temple hall, named for a Muromachi-era Jizo hall.

832.description_ja_long: 東京都八王子市と神奈川県相模原市の境に立つ標高855mの山。白馬の像が立つ広い山頂は360度の展望で知られ、高尾山へ続く奥高尾縦走路の起点となる。
832.description_ja_short: 白馬の像が立つ山頂から360度の眺め。奥高尾の名峰。
832.description_en_long: An 855 m mountain on the border of Hachioji, Tokyo, and Sagamihara, Kanagawa. Its broad summit, marked by a statue of a white horse, is famed for 360-degree views and forms the starting point of the Oku-Takao ridge route leading to Mt. Takao.
832.description_en_short: A 360-degree panorama from a summit with a white horse statue; a noted peak of Oku-Takao.

833.description_ja_long: 八丈島の西にそびえる伊豆七島の最高峰。駿河の富士に似た端正な姿からこの名で呼ばれ、1606年の噴火を最後に静まる火山。標高854m。
833.description_ja_short: 駿河の富士を思わせる姿の伊豆七島最高峰。八丈島の火山。
833.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Izu Seven Islands, rising on the western side of Hachijojima. Called Hachijo-Fuji for its shapely form recalling Mt. Fuji in Suruga, this 854 m volcano has stayed quiet since its last eruption in 1606.
833.description_en_short: The Izu Seven Islands' highest peak, a Hachijojima volcano shaped like the Fuji of Suruga.

834.description_ja_long: 岩手県二戸市と九戸村などにまたがる北上山地北部の山。牛が臥せたような穏やかな山容で、山頂からは岩手山や七時雨山など北の山々を広く見渡す。標高852m。
834.description_ja_short: 牛が臥せたような姿の北上山地の山。頂から岩手山を望む。
834.description_en_long: A mountain of the northern Kitakami Highlands straddling Ninohe City, Kunohe Village and beyond in Iwate. Its gentle profile suggests a reclining ox, and the 852 m summit takes in a wide sweep of northern peaks such as Mt. Iwate and Nanashigureyama.
834.description_en_short: A Kitakami Highlands peak shaped like a reclining ox, with Mt. Iwate seen from the top.

835.description_ja_long: 新潟県村上市、蒲萄山塊の最高峰。一等三角点の頂は飯豊・朝日連峰から鳥海山まで見渡し、日本海には粟島が浮かぶ。ブナ林に花咲く春が美しい。標高852m。
835.description_ja_short: 蒲萄山塊の最高峰。頂から飯豊・朝日を望み、日本海に粟島が浮かぶ。
835.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Budo mountain mass in Murakami, Niigata, rising 852 m. Its first-order triangulation summit surveys the Iide and Asahi ranges through to Mt. Chokai, with Awashima Island afloat on the Sea of Japan. Spring blossoms grace its beech woods.
835.description_en_short: Highest of the Budo mountains; the top views Iide and Asahi, with Awashima on the Sea of Japan.

836.description_ja_long: 埼玉県飯能市、奥武蔵を代表する標高851mの人気峰。山頂北側に長い鎖の岩場・男坂を擁し、南隣の御岳には見事な広葉樹の森が広がる。
836.description_ja_short: 奥武蔵を代表する人気の山。鎖の岩場・男坂で知られる。
836.description_en_long: A popular 851 m peak in Hanno, Saitama, representative of the Oku-Musashi area. The Otoko-zaka, a long chained rock face, lies on the north side of the summit, while a splendid broadleaf forest spreads over Mitake, its neighbor to the south.
836.description_en_short: A popular signature peak of Oku-Musashi, known for the chained rock face of Otoko-zaka.

837.description_ja_long: 絶海の孤島・御蔵島の最高峰で、伊豆諸島では2番目の高さを誇る。巨樹の森や海鳥の繁殖地、固有の生きものなど手つかずの自然に包まれる。標高851m。
837.description_ja_short: 伊豆諸島で2番目の高さ、巨樹の森と海鳥が息づく御蔵島の最高峰。
837.description_en_long: The highest point of Mikurajima, a solitary island in the open sea, and the second-highest summit in the Izu Islands. The 851 m mountain is wrapped in untouched nature: forests of giant trees, seabird breeding grounds, and creatures found nowhere else.
837.description_en_short: Mikurajima's highest peak, second in the Izu Islands, alive with giant trees and seabirds.

838.description_ja_long: 京都と滋賀の境にそびえる標高848mの霊山。最澄が開いた天台宗総本山・延暦寺を擁し、都富士とも呼ばれる均整のとれた姿が古都を見守ってきた。
838.description_ja_short: 最澄開山の延暦寺を擁する霊山。都富士とも呼ばれる。
838.description_en_long: A sacred 848 m mountain rising on the border of Kyoto and Shiga, home to Enryakuji, head temple of the Tendai school founded by Saicho. Its well-balanced form, nicknamed the 'Fuji of the capital,' has watched over the old capital through the ages.
838.description_en_short: A sacred mountain bearing Saicho's temple Enryakuji, nicknamed the 'Fuji of the capital.'

839.description_ja_long: 三重県にある標高842mの山。南東の山麓に山名を冠した航空自衛隊笠取山分屯基地が広がり、防空を担う基地の山として知られる。
839.description_ja_short: 南東山麓に航空自衛隊の分屯基地が広がる三重県の山。
839.description_en_long: An 842 m mountain in Mie Prefecture. Across its southeastern foot spreads the Japan Air Self-Defense Force's Kasatoriyama Sub-Base, which takes its name from the peak, and the mountain is known for this air-defense installation.
839.description_en_short: A Mie mountain with a JASDF sub-base named after it spreading over its southeastern foot.

840.description_ja_long: 北海道北部、枝幸町と中頓別町にまたがる標高841mの山。石灰岩でできた山として知られ、その特異な環境が珍しい草花を育んでいる。
840.description_ja_short: 石灰岩でできた北海道北部の山。珍しい草花の姿が楽しめる。
840.description_en_long: An 841 m mountain in northern Hokkaido straddling the towns of Esashi and Nakatombetsu. It is known as a mountain built of limestone, and this distinctive environment nurtures wildflowers that are rarely seen elsewhere.
840.description_en_short: A limestone mountain in northern Hokkaido where rare wildflowers can be enjoyed.

841.description_ja_long: 兵庫県豊岡市と朝来市の境、床尾連山の西端に立つ標高839mの一等三角点峰。谷が深く「迷えば二度と出られぬ」と恐れられ、頂からは氷ノ山や大江山を望む。
841.description_ja_short: 床尾連山西端の一等三角点峰。深い谷の伝承で知られる。
841.description_en_long: An 839 m first-order triangulation peak at the western end of the Tokonoo Range on the border of Toyooka and Asago, Hyogo. Its valleys run so deep that people feared 'once lost, you never come out,' and the summit looks out to Hyonosen and Oeyama.
841.description_en_short: First-order triangulation peak at the Tokonoo Range's west end, known for its deep-valley lore.

842.description_ja_long: 広島県呉市に広がる標高839mの高原状の山塊で、瀬戸内海国立公園では六甲山に次ぐ高さ。頂上台地から瀬戸内の島々と四国の山並みを一望する。
842.description_ja_short: 頂上の台地から瀬戸内海の島々を一望する、呉の広大な山塊。
842.description_en_long: A plateau-like mountain mass of 839 m spreading across Kure, Hiroshima, second in height only to Mt. Rokko within Setonaikai National Park. From its summit plateau, the islands of the Seto Inland Sea and the mountains of Shikoku unfold in a single view.
842.description_en_short: A vast mountain mass in Kure whose summit plateau surveys the islands of the Seto Inland Sea.

843.description_ja_long: 広島県神石郡の町境に立つ一等三角点の山。孝徳天皇の御代、天から星が降り七里四方を照らしたという伝説が名の由来で、山頂近くには星ノ池が水をたたえる。
843.description_ja_short: 星が降った伝説が名に残る一等三角点の山。頂近くに星ノ池。
843.description_en_long: A first-order triangulation peak standing on a town border in Jinseki District, Hiroshima. The name comes from a legend that in the reign of Emperor Kotoku a star fell from the heavens and lit the land for seven ri around; near the summit lies the pond Hoshinoike.
843.description_en_short: A triangulation peak named for a legend of a fallen star, with the pond Hoshinoike near the top.

844.description_ja_long: 静岡県島田市と掛川市の境に立つ一等三角点の山。標高832mながら山頂の眺めは広く、富士山から双耳峰の笊ヶ岳まで望み、眼下には大井川が悠然と流れる。
844.description_ja_short: 富士山や笊ヶ岳を望む一等三角点の低山。眼下に大井川。
844.description_en_long: A first-order triangulation peak on the border of Shimada and Kakegawa in Shizuoka. Though only 832 m, the summit view is broad, reaching from Mt. Fuji to the twin-peaked Zarugatake, while the Oi River flows grandly below.
844.description_en_short: A low first-order triangulation peak viewing Mt. Fuji and Zarugatake, the Oi River below.

845.description_ja_long: 丹後山地の中央に連なる標高832mの連山で、最高峰が千丈ヶ嶽。源頼光の酒呑童子退治の伝説の舞台として名高く、雲海の名所としても知られる。
845.description_ja_short: 酒呑童子伝説の舞台。雲海の名所として知られる丹後の連山。
845.description_en_long: An 832 m chain of peaks running through the heart of the Tango Mountains, crowned by its highest point, Senjogatake. Famed as the stage of the legend in which Minamoto no Yorimitsu slew the demon Shuten-doji, it is also renowned for its seas of clouds.
845.description_en_short: The Tango range famed for the Shuten-doji demon legend and for its seas of clouds.

846.description_ja_long: 宮崎県延岡市にそびえる岩峰で、雄岳と雌岳の間に行縢の滝がかかる。山名は姿が武者の臑当「むかばき」に似ることに由来し、日本武尊の伝説も残る。標高830m。
846.description_ja_short: 武者の臑当に似た岩峰。雄岳と雌岳の間に滝がかかる。
846.description_en_long: Mukabakiyama is an 830 m rocky peak rising in Nobeoka, Miyazaki, with Mukabaki Falls hanging between its twin summits, Odake and Medake. The name comes from its resemblance to a warrior's shin guard called mukabaki, and legends of Yamato Takeru also linger here.
846.description_en_short: A rocky peak shaped like a warrior's shin guard, with a waterfall hanging between its summits Odake and Medake.

847.description_ja_long: 北海道北見市と佐呂間町にまたがる標高829mの山。周囲に高い山が少ないため独り立ちした姿が目を引き、一等三角点の頂は全方位の展望に恵まれる。
847.description_ja_short: 周囲に高山がなく際立つ姿の山。頂から四方に展望が広がる。
847.description_en_long: Nikoroyama is an 829 m mountain straddling Kitami and Saroma in Hokkaido. With few high peaks around it, its solitary figure catches the eye, and the summit, marked by a first-order triangulation point, is blessed with sweeping views in every direction.
847.description_en_short: A mountain standing out with no high peaks around it; the summit opens panoramic views on all sides.

848.description_ja_long: 福島県伊達市と相馬市の境に岩肌をそびやかす標高825mの名勝。平安初期に開かれ「北の叡山」と栄えた山岳霊場で、南北朝期には霊山城の激戦の舞台となった。
848.description_ja_short: 「北の叡山」と栄えた岩山。紅葉と断崖の景観が見事。
848.description_en_long: Ryozen is an 825 m scenic mountain thrusting rocky walls above the border of Date and Soma in Fukushima. Opened in the early Heian period, it flourished as a sacred site called the 'Hiei of the North' and saw fierce battles over Ryozen Castle in the Nanbokucho era.
848.description_en_short: A rocky mountain that flourished as the 'Hiei of the North,' with superb scenery of autumn leaves and cliffs.

850.description_ja_long: 北海道北部、紋別市と興部町、西興部村、滝上町の4市町村の境に頭をもたげる山。標高818m、頂には一等三角点「欝岳」が据えられている。
850.description_ja_short: 道北の4市町村境に立つ山。山頂に一等三角点を戴く。
850.description_en_long: Utsudake raises its head at the meeting point of four municipalities in northern Hokkaido: Monbetsu, Okoppe, Nishiokoppe and Takinoue. The mountain stands 818 m high, and a first-order triangulation point named 'Utsudake' is set on its summit.
850.description_en_short: A mountain on the border of four municipalities in northern Hokkaido, crowned by a first-order triangulation point.

853.description_ja_long: 愛媛県西部、大洲市と八幡浜市などの境に立つ標高812mの山。頂に立つ出石寺は奈良時代開創と伝わる名刹で、境内から瀬戸内の海と霧に浮かぶ石鎚連峰を望む。
853.description_ja_short: 頂に古刹・出石寺が立つ山。瀬戸内海と石鎚連峰を望む。
853.description_en_long: Izushiyama is an 812 m mountain on the border of Ozu, Yawatahama and other towns in western Ehime. Izushiji, the noted temple on its summit, is said to date from the Nara period, and its grounds overlook the Seto Inland Sea and the Ishizuchi Range floating in mist.
853.description_en_short: A mountain crowned by the old temple Izushiji, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea and the Ishizuchi Range.

854.description_ja_long: 日高の海岸近くに立つ標高810mの山。かんらん岩の山体ゆえ低くても高山植物の宝庫で、その群落は国の特別天然記念物。アポイの名を冠す花も多い。
854.description_ja_short: かんらん岩が育む高山植物の宝庫。花の名峰として名高い。
854.description_en_long: Mt. Apoi is an 810 m mountain rising near the Hidaka coast of Hokkaido. Thanks to its peridotite body, it is a treasure house of alpine plants despite its modest height, its communities designated a Special Natural Monument, and many flowers carry the name Apoi.
854.description_en_short: A treasure house of alpine plants nurtured by peridotite, celebrated as a famous mountain of flowers.

855.description_ja_long: 島根県大田市南西部、大代の里のシンボルと親しまれる標高808mの火山性の山。山頂からは日本海や三瓶山を望み、屏風岩を仰ぐ道にはアセビが群れ咲く。
855.description_ja_short: 大代の里のシンボル。山頂から三瓶山や日本海を望む。
855.description_en_long: Oetakayama is an 808 m volcanic mountain in southwestern Oda, Shimane, cherished as the symbol of the village of Odai. The summit looks out to the Sea of Japan and Mt. Sanbe, and Japanese andromeda blooms in masses along the trail beneath the Byobuiwa rock face.
855.description_en_short: The symbol of the village of Odai, its summit overlooking Mt. Sanbe and the Sea of Japan.

857.description_ja_long: 筑紫平野に東西へ横たわる耳納山地の最高峰で標高802m。山頂は山城の跡で、360度の展望が広がり、筑後の平野と山々をぐるりと見渡せる。
857.description_ja_short: 耳納山地の最高峰。山城跡の頂から360度の展望が広がる。
857.description_en_long: Takatoriyama, at 802 m, is the highest peak of the Minou Mountains stretching east to west along the Chikushi Plain. Its summit, the site of a former mountain castle, opens a full 360-degree panorama over the plains and mountains of the Chikugo region.
857.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Minou Mountains, with a 360-degree panorama from its castle-site summit.

858.description_ja_long: 蔵王連峰の東に構える宮城県の独立峰で、どっしりとした双耳の姿は蔵王と見まがうほど。古くは大刈田山と呼ばれ、刈田嶺の噴火を鎮めるため遥拝された山と伝わる。
858.description_ja_short: 蔵王と見まがう双耳の独立峰。噴火鎮めの遥拝の山と伝わる。
858.description_en_long: Aosoyama is a solitary peak in Miyagi standing east of the Zao range, its massive twin-peaked figure easily mistaken for Zao itself. Once called Okaridayama, it is said to have been worshipped from afar as a mountain to calm the eruptions of Kattamine.
858.description_en_short: A twin-peaked solitary mountain easily mistaken for Zao, said to have been worshipped to quell eruptions.

859.description_ja_long: 伊豆半島北東部、熱海市などにまたがる標高798mの山。古い多賀火山の名残の峰で、芝に覆われた山頂からは富士山や駿河湾、天城の山々の大観が広がる。
859.description_ja_short: 芝の山頂から富士山と駿河湾を一望する伊豆の展望峰。
859.description_en_long: Kurotake is a 798 m mountain straddling Atami and neighboring areas in the northeastern Izu Peninsula. A remnant peak of the old Taga volcano, its grass-covered summit spreads a grand panorama of Mt. Fuji, Suruga Bay and the mountains of Amagi.
859.description_en_short: A viewpoint peak of Izu whose grassy summit commands Mt. Fuji and Suruga Bay.

860.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県トカラ列島の諏訪之瀬島にそびえる標高796mの火山。今も小規模な噴火が続き、島の暮らしのすぐそばで火山活動を見せ続ける山である。
860.description_ja_short: トカラ列島・諏訪之瀬島の火山。今も小さな噴火を続ける。
860.description_en_long: Otake is a 796 m active volcano soaring on Suwanosejima in the Tokara Islands of Kagoshima. Small eruptions continue even now, and the mountain keeps displaying its volcanic activity right beside the daily life of the islanders.
860.description_en_short: An active volcano on Suwanosejima in the Tokara Islands, still producing small eruptions today.

861.description_ja_long: 北海道渡島半島の突端近くにひっそりと立つ標高794mの山。派手な逸話は伝わらず、半島最果ての静けさをまとう、知る人ぞ知る一座である。
861.description_ja_short: 北海道渡島半島の突端近くに構える標高794mの静かな山。
861.description_en_long: Iwabedake is a 794 m mountain standing quietly near the tip of the Oshima Peninsula in Hokkaido. No colorful tales are attached to it; wrapped in the stillness of the peninsula's far end, it remains a peak known only to those in the know.
861.description_en_short: A quiet 794 m mountain standing near the tip of Hokkaido's Oshima Peninsula.

862.description_ja_long: 福井県越前市と南越前町の境に立つ標高794mの山。平野から仰ぐ富士山形の姿は越前富士と呼ばれ、山頂に日野三所権現を祭る信仰の山として敬われてきた。
862.description_ja_short: 富士山形の姿から越前富士と呼ばれる信仰の山。山頂に権現を祭る。
862.description_en_long: Hinosan is a 794 m mountain on the border of Echizen City and Minamiechizen in Fukui. Its Fuji-like cone seen from the plain has earned it the name Echizen Fuji, and it has long been revered as a mountain of faith, with the Hino Sansho Gongen enshrined on its summit.
862.description_en_short: A mountain of faith called Echizen Fuji for its Fuji-like form, with a gongen shrine on its summit.

863.description_ja_long: 兵庫県篠山の多紀アルプス最高峰。かつて修験道で栄えたが、大和大峰山に従わなかったため寺を焼かれ衰えたと伝わる。一等三角点の頂は展望に優れる。
863.description_ja_short: 多紀アルプスの最高峰。修験の栄枯を伝える展望の名山。
863.description_en_long: Mitake is the highest peak of the Taki Alps in Sasayama, Hyogo. It once flourished with Shugendo, but its temples were reportedly burned and it declined after refusing to submit to Mt. Omine in Yamato. Its first-order triangulation summit offers fine views.
863.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Taki Alps, a scenic summit that tells of the rise and fall of Shugendo.

865.description_ja_long: 愛知県東三河にそびえる三河富士。三河国一ノ宮・砥鹿神社の奥宮が山頂近くに鎮座する信仰の山で、杉の巨木「天狗の寄木」が往時の深山の面影を伝える。
865.description_ja_short: 三河富士と呼ばれる信仰の山。砥鹿神社奥宮が鎮座する。
865.description_en_long: Hongusan, known as Mikawa Fuji, rises in eastern Mikawa, Aichi. A mountain of faith, it holds the inner shrine of Toga Shrine, first shrine of old Mikawa Province, near the summit, while a giant cedar called 'Tengu no Yoriki' recalls the deep forests of former days.
865.description_en_short: A mountain of faith called Mikawa Fuji, where the inner shrine of Toga Shrine is enshrined.

866.description_ja_long: 高松市南東、讃岐山脈前衛の山。南北二つのピークが弓の矢筈のように並ぶ姿が名の由来で、南麓には四国八十八ヶ所結願の寺・大窪寺が静かに構える。
866.description_ja_short: 二つの峰が矢筈をなす山。麓に結願の寺・大窪寺が建つ。
866.description_en_long: Yahazuyama stands southeast of Takamatsu as an outlying peak of the Sanuki Range. Its name comes from its two peaks aligned north and south like the nock of an arrow, and at its southern foot quietly stands Okuboji, the final temple of the Shikoku 88-temple pilgrimage.
866.description_en_short: A mountain whose two peaks form an arrow nock, with Okuboji, the pilgrimage's final temple, at its foot.

867.description_ja_long: 福井県若狭町と美浜町の境に横たわる標高786mの山。西麓には手足の不自由や怪我にご利益があると伝わる片手観音像で名高い三方石観音が祀られている。
867.description_ja_short: 若狭町と美浜町の境の山。麓に片手観音で名高い三方石観音。
867.description_en_long: Kumodaniyama is a 786 m mountain lying on the border of Wakasa and Mihama in Fukui. At its foot stands Mikata Ishikannon, famed for its one-handed Kannon image, which is believed to bring blessings for injuries and impairments of the hands and feet.
867.description_en_short: A mountain on the Wakasa-Mihama border; at its foot is Mikata Ishikannon, famed for its one-handed Kannon.

868.description_ja_long: 大阪府最北部、能勢に構える北摂随一の雄峰。山腹の岩には磨崖仏が刻まれ、山頂近くには月峰寺跡が残るなど、修験の山だった往時の面影を色濃く伝える。
868.description_ja_short: 磨崖仏や月峰寺跡が修験の歴史を伝える、展望に優れた北摂の雄峰。
868.description_en_long: Kenbisan, in Nose at the northern tip of Osaka Prefecture, is a commanding peak of the Hokusetsu region. Buddhist images carved into rock faces on its slopes and the ruins of Geppoji temple near the summit vividly recall its days as a mountain of Shugendo practice.
868.description_en_short: A commanding Hokusetsu peak with fine views, where rock-carved Buddhas and temple ruins tell of Shugendo.

869.description_ja_long: 青森県下北半島に横たわる標高781mの火山。二百万〜一万年前の噴火で生まれた山体は平坦で、その姿から佐藤ヶ平山という飾らない別名でも呼ばれる。
869.description_ja_short: 下北半島の平らな火山。別名は佐藤ヶ平山。標高781m。
869.description_en_long: Hiuchidake is a 781 m volcano lying on the Shimokita Peninsula of Aomori. Built by eruptions between two million and ten thousand years ago, its body is notably flat, and this shape has earned it the unadorned alternative name of Satogataiyama.
869.description_en_short: A flat volcano on the Shimokita Peninsula, 781 m high, also known by the plain name Satogataiyama.

870.description_ja_long: 志摩半島の付け根、度会山地の中核をなす標高778mの山。ケイビランの自生群落で知られ、一等三角点の頂からは清流宮川や熊野灘までも見渡せる。
870.description_ja_short: ケイビランが自生する度会山地の主峰。一等三角点の山。
870.description_en_long: Nanahoradake, 778 m, is the core of the Watarai Mountains at the base of the Shima Peninsula. Known for wild colonies of the keibiran lily, it bears a first-order triangulation point on its summit, from which the clear Miyagawa river and even the Kumano Sea are visible.
870.description_en_short: The main peak of the Watarai Mountains, where keibiran lilies grow wild; a first-order triangulation summit.

871.description_ja_long: 長崎県佐世保市と佐賀県有田町の境に立つ山。佐賀側は急峻な岩稜、長崎側はなだらかな玄武岩の台地と二つの顔をもち、頂からは五島列島や阿蘇まで望む。
871.description_ja_short: 佐賀側は岩稜、長崎側は台地。二つの顔をもつ県境の山。
871.description_en_long: Kunimiyama stands on the border of Sasebo in Nagasaki and Arita in Saga. It shows two faces, a steep rocky ridge on the Saga side and a gentle basalt plateau on the Nagasaki side, and from its summit the view reaches as far as the Goto Islands and Aso.
871.description_en_short: A border mountain with two faces: a rocky ridge on the Saga side, a gentle plateau on the Nagasaki side.

872.description_ja_long: 伊豆諸島・三宅島の主峰をなす標高775mの活火山。度重なる噴火は森を焼き、新澪池の水を一気に蒸発させるなど、島の風景を幾度も塗り替えてきた。
872.description_ja_short: 三宅島の活火山。噴火が島の風景を幾度も塗り替えてきた。
872.description_en_long: Oyama is a 775 m active volcano forming the main peak of Miyakejima in the Izu Islands. Its repeated eruptions have burned forests and even evaporated the waters of Shinmyo Pond in one stroke, transforming the island's landscape again and again.
872.description_en_short: The active volcano of Miyakejima, whose eruptions have repeatedly reshaped the island's landscape.

873.description_ja_long: 徳島市の最高峰で、四国山地主稜の東端を飾る秀峰。日峰山・津峰山とともに阿波三峰と呼ばれ、その灯は海をゆく船の道しるべとして信仰を集めてきた。
873.description_ja_short: 阿波三峰の一つ。灯が船を導く海の守護神として信仰される。
873.description_en_long: Nakatsuminesan is the highest peak in Tokushima City, a graceful mountain at the eastern end of the main ridge of the Shikoku Mountains. With Hinomine and Tsunomine it forms the Three Peaks of Awa, and its light has long been worshipped as a guide for ships at sea.
873.description_en_short: One of the Three Peaks of Awa, worshipped as a sea guardian whose light guides ships on their way.

874.description_ja_long: 滋賀と三重の県境、鈴鹿山脈の南端に位置する標高773mの山。灌木が切り払われた広い山頂からは、遮るもののない360度の大展望が広がる。
874.description_ja_short: 鈴鹿山脈南端の山。広い頂から360度の展望が広がる。
874.description_en_long: Takahatayama is a 773 m mountain at the southern end of the Suzuka Range, on the border of Shiga and Mie. The shrubs on its broad summit have been cleared away, opening an unobstructed 360-degree panorama with nothing to block the view.
874.description_en_short: A peak at the southern end of the Suzuka Range; its broad summit opens a 360-degree panorama.

875.description_ja_long: 高知県須崎市の山。大蛇が乙女に化けて人を惑わせたという伝説を名に負う一方、麓には桃の花に彩られる桃源郷めいた里が広がり、頂の展望も素晴らしい。
875.description_ja_short: 大蛇伝説を名に負う高知の山。麓に桃の花咲く桃源郷の里。
875.description_en_long: Bandagamori is a mountain in Susaki, Kochi. Its name carries a legend of a giant serpent that turned into a maiden to beguile people, while at its foot spreads a village colored by peach blossoms like a hidden paradise, and the view from the summit is superb.
875.description_en_short: A Kochi mountain named for a serpent legend, with a peach-blossom village like a paradise at its foot.

876.description_ja_long: 三重県布引山地の北部に立つ標高766mの山。釈迦が法華経を説いた霊鷲山に姿が似ることが名の由来とされ、頂には寺院跡やアセビの群落が残る。
876.description_ja_short: 霊鷲山に似ることが名の由来。頂に寺院跡とアセビの群落。
876.description_en_long: A 766 m mountain in the northern Nunobiki Mountains of Mie Prefecture. Its name is said to come from its resemblance to Vulture Peak, where the Buddha preached the Lotus Sutra, and temple ruins and thickets of asebi shrubs remain on its summit.
876.description_en_short: Named for its likeness to Vulture Peak; temple ruins and asebi thickets remain on the summit.

877.description_ja_long: 伊豆大島の中央に鎮座する活火山。1986年の大噴火では溶岩が集落に迫り全島避難となった。今は火口を巡る道が整い、海と富士を望む展望が広がる。
877.description_ja_short: 伊豆大島の活火山。噴火を繰り返し、火口を巡る道が通う。
877.description_en_long: An active volcano seated at the center of Izu Oshima island. In the great eruption of 1986, lava pressed toward the villages and the whole island was evacuated. Today a well-kept trail circles the crater, opening views over the sea to Mt. Fuji.
877.description_en_short: An active volcano on Izu Oshima that has erupted repeatedly; a trail now circles its crater.

878.description_ja_long: 大分県、耶馬渓の一角にそびえる雄岳と雌岳の双耳峰。集塊岩が生む奇観で知られ、万里の長城にたとえられる「地蔵峠の景」やシャクナゲの群落が彩る。
878.description_ja_short: 耶馬渓の双耳峰。集塊岩の奇観「地蔵峠の景」で知られる。
878.description_en_long: Twin peaks, Odake and Medake, rising in a corner of the Yabakei area of Oita Prefecture. The mountain is known for strange rock scenery formed of agglomerate, graced by the 'Jizo Pass view' likened to the Great Wall of China and by groves of rhododendrons.
878.description_en_short: Twin peaks of Yabakei, known for the agglomerate rock scenery of the 'Jizo Pass view'.

879.description_ja_long: 三重県松阪市、市街に迫る高見山地の一峰で標高757m。局ヶ岳・白猪山とともに伊勢三山と呼ばれ、秋葉大権現を祭る頂は月見の名所となるほど眺めがよい。
879.description_ja_short: 伊勢三山の一つ。とがった頂は月見の名所となる好展望。
879.description_en_long: A 757 m peak of the Takami Mountains rising close to the city of Matsusaka, Mie Prefecture. With Tsubonegatake and Shiraisan it is counted among the Three Mountains of Ise, and its summit, enshrining Akiba Daigongen, is a famed moon-viewing spot with fine views.
879.description_en_short: One of the Three Mountains of Ise; its pointed summit is a moon-viewing spot with fine views.

880.description_ja_long: 富山県南砺市の市街の南に横たわる標高756mの山。山頂近くに風神堂が祀られ、毎年6月には風を鎮め豊作を祈る祭りが営まれる風の信仰の山だ。
880.description_ja_short: 山頂近くに風神堂。風を鎮め豊作を祈る祭りが伝わる山。
880.description_en_long: A 756 m mountain lying south of the town center of Nanto, Toyama Prefecture. A shrine to the wind god stands near the summit, and every June a festival is held to calm the winds and pray for a good harvest, making this a mountain of wind worship.
880.description_en_short: A wind-god shrine stands near the summit, where a festival prays for calm winds and good harvests.

881.description_ja_long: 紀ノ川南岸にそびえる紀州富士。名の龍門は雨乞いに通じ、農事信仰の山であった。頂にはキイシモツケが群れ咲き、春はギフチョウが舞う自然の宝庫だ。
881.description_ja_short: 紀州富士と呼ばれる雨乞い信仰の山。キイシモツケ咲く。
881.description_en_long: Rising on the south bank of the Kinokawa River and called the Kishu Fuji, this was a mountain of rainmaking and farming faith, its name Ryumon evoking the dragon. Kiishimotsuke spiraea blooms on the summit and gifu butterflies dance in spring, a treasury of nature.
881.description_en_short: Called the Kishu Fuji, a mountain of rainmaking faith where Kiishimotsuke spiraea blooms.

882.description_ja_long: 大分県豊後大野市と佐伯市の境に立つ標高754mの山。一等三角点を置く頂の見晴らしはよく、由布・鶴見の峰々から祖母山までを一望におさめる。
882.description_ja_short: 一等三角点の頂から由布や祖母山を一望する大分百山の一座。
882.description_en_long: A 754 m mountain standing on the border of Bungo-ono and Saiki in Oita Prefecture. Its summit, which holds a first-order triangulation point, offers a fine panorama sweeping from the peaks of Yufu and Tsurumi all the way to Mt. Sobo.
882.description_en_short: From its summit with a first-order triangulation point, views reach from Yufu to Mt. Sobo.

884.description_ja_long: 山口盆地の北西に構え、東鳳翩山と向かい合ってそびえる山。標高では東鳳翩山にまさるものの、頂には鉄塔が林立し、静かに盆地を見下ろし続けている。
884.description_ja_short: 東鳳翩山と対をなし山口盆地を見下ろす山。標高は742m。
884.description_en_long: A mountain standing northwest of the Yamaguchi Basin, facing Higashi-Hobenzan across the way. Though it surpasses its eastern partner in height, its summit bristles with steel transmission towers, and it quietly keeps watch over the basin below.
884.description_en_short: Paired with Higashi-Hobenzan, it looks down on the Yamaguchi Basin from a height of 742 m.

886.description_ja_long: 北上山地の北端、青森・岩手の県境に牛が伏せたような山容を横たえる。別名臥牛山。頂に山の神と龍神を祭り、湧き出す龍神水は名水として親しまれる。
886.description_ja_short: 牛が伏せたような姿から臥牛山とも。頂に龍神水が湧く。
886.description_en_long: Lying like a reclining ox on the Aomori-Iwate border at the northern end of the Kitakami Highlands, it is also called Gagyuzan. A mountain god and a dragon god are enshrined on the summit, and the Ryujin spring water that wells up there is prized as excellent water.
886.description_en_short: Also called Gagyuzan for its reclining-ox form; the Ryujin spring wells up near the summit.

887.description_ja_long: 山形県、朝日山地の周辺に連なる標高736mの山。県内の名峰を選んだ「やまがた百名山」にも数えられ、地元で親しまれてきた一座である。
887.description_ja_short: 朝日山地周辺に立つ標高736mの山。やまがた百名山の一つ。
887.description_en_long: A 736 m mountain in the ranges around the Asahi Mountains in Yamagata Prefecture. It is counted among the 100 Famous Mountains of Yamagata, a selection of the prefecture's notable peaks, and has long been cherished by the local people.
887.description_en_short: A 736 m peak near the Asahi Mountains, one of the 100 Famous Mountains of Yamagata.

889.description_ja_long: 洞爺湖の南に構える標高733mの活火山。江戸期の記録に頂が臼の形に焼け崩れると記され、国学者・菅江真澄も登った。噴火のたびに姿を変える山として知られる。
889.description_ja_short: 洞爺湖の南に立つ活火山。噴火のたびに山頂の姿を変えてきた。
889.description_en_long: Mt. Usu is a 733 m active volcano standing south of Lake Toya. Edo-period records describe its summit as burned and collapsed into the shape of a mortar, and the scholar Sugae Masumi once climbed it. It is known as a mountain that changes its form with every eruption.
889.description_en_short: An active volcano south of Lake Toya whose summit has changed shape with each eruption.

890.description_ja_long: 松前の沖50kmに浮かぶ無人の火山島、渡島大島の最高点。標高732m。島はオオミズナギドリの繁殖地として天然記念物となり、人を寄せつけぬまま海に聳える。
890.description_ja_short: 松前沖の無人火山島の最高点。海鳥が群れ棲む天然記念物の島。
890.description_en_long: The highest point of Oshima Oshima, an uninhabited volcanic island floating 50 km off Matsumae, rising 732 m above the sea. The island, a breeding ground for streaked shearwaters, is designated a natural monument and towers over the water untouched by humans.
890.description_en_short: High point of an uninhabited volcanic island off Matsumae, a natural monument thronged by seabirds.

891.description_ja_long: 岩手県、北上高地の山並みの一角に位置する標高731mの山。十二の神を戴くかのような山名が目を引き、静かな山域にひっそりと立つ知る人ぞ知る一峰。
891.description_ja_short: 北上高地に佇む一峰。十二の神を思わせる名が印象深い。
891.description_en_long: A 731 m mountain in a corner of the Kitakami Highlands of Iwate Prefecture. Its striking name, suggesting a mountain crowned by twelve gods, catches the eye, and it stands quietly in a tranquil range as a peak known only to those in the know.
891.description_en_short: A quiet peak of the Kitakami Highlands, memorable for a name evoking twelve gods.

892.description_ja_long: 北海道留萌地方、留萌市と小平町の境に立つ標高730mの山。アイヌ語で「大きい山」を意味する名を持ちながら穏やかな低山で、頂には一等三角点が据えられている。
892.description_ja_short: アイヌ語で「大きい」の名を持つ留萌の低山。一等三角点の頂。
892.description_en_long: A 730 m mountain on the border of Rumoi City and the town of Obira in Hokkaido's Rumoi region. Though its Ainu name means 'big mountain,' it is a gentle low peak, and a first-order triangulation point is set on its summit.
892.description_en_short: A low Rumoi mountain whose Ainu name means 'big,' with a first-order triangulation point on top.

893.description_ja_long: 丹波篠山盆地の南西に秀麗な姿でそびえ、丹波富士とも呼ばれる標高722mの山。露岩の目立つ頂は展望が雄大で、隣の松尾山には中世の山城や古寺の跡が眠る。
893.description_ja_short: 丹波富士と称される秀峰。露岩の頂から雄大な展望が広がる。
893.description_en_long: A 722 m mountain rising in graceful form southwest of the Tanba-Sasayama Basin, also known as the Tanba Fuji. Its rocky summit commands a magnificent panorama, while on neighboring Matsuoyama lie the ruins of a medieval hilltop castle and an old temple.
893.description_en_short: A shapely peak called the Tanba Fuji; grand views open from its rock-strewn summit.

894.description_ja_long: 大分県国東半島の中央にそびえる最高峰で標高720m。半島は百万年以上前の火山活動が生んだ地形で、山麓には六郷満山と呼ばれる寺院群が点在する仏の里である。
894.description_ja_short: 国東半島の最高峰。麓に六郷満山の寺院群が点在する。
894.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Kunisaki Peninsula in Oita Prefecture, rising 720 m at its center. The peninsula's terrain was born of volcanic activity more than a million years ago, and its foothills are dotted with the temples of Rokugo Manzan, a storied Buddhist heartland.
894.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Kunisaki Peninsula, with the Rokugo Manzan temples dotting its foothills.

895.description_ja_long: 津軽半島の最高峰で標高718m。どっしりと構えた山容は陸奥湾を行く船からよく映える。深いヤブに包まれた頂からは津軽海峡越しに北海道までも見渡せる。
895.description_ja_short: 津軽半島の最高峰。海峡の彼方に北海道を望む重厚な山。
895.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Tsugaru Peninsula at 718 m. Its massive, settled form shows handsomely to ships crossing Mutsu Bay, and from the summit, wrapped in deep brush, the view reaches across the Tsugaru Strait as far as Hokkaido.
895.description_en_short: Highest point of the Tsugaru Peninsula, a stately mountain looking across the strait to Hokkaido.

896.description_ja_long: 男鹿半島にそびえ、真山・毛無山とともに男鹿三山をなす標高715mの山。古くから赤神権現を祀る修験の道場で、麓には鬼が一夜で築いたと伝わる999段の石段が残る。
896.description_ja_short: 男鹿三山の一峰。鬼が一夜で築いたという石段の伝説が残る。
896.description_en_long: A 715 m mountain on the Oga Peninsula forming the Oga Sanzan together with Shinzan and Kenashiyama. Long a shugendo training ground enshrining Akagami Gongen, it keeps at its foot a flight of 999 stone steps said to have been built by an ogre in a single night.
896.description_en_short: One of the three Oga Sanzan peaks, with a legend of stone steps an ogre built in one night.

897.description_ja_long: 秋田県由利本荘市、出羽山地にひときわ美しくそびえる標高713mの山。ブナの原生林に包まれ、頂近くの神社前からは秀峰鳥海山を間近に仰ぐ絶好の展望が開ける。
897.description_ja_short: ブナ林に包まれた出羽山地の山。鳥海山を仰ぐ展望が見事。
897.description_en_long: A 713 m mountain rising with singular beauty in the Dewa Hills of Yurihonjo, Akita Prefecture. Cloaked in virgin beech forest, it offers, from in front of a shrine near the summit, a superb close-up view of the graceful peak of Mt. Chokai.
897.description_en_short: A beech-clad mountain of the Dewa Hills with a splendid close-up view of Mt. Chokai.

898.description_ja_long: 日本三大カルストに数えられる平尾台の北端に立つ貫山地の主峰で標高712m。羊の群れのように白い石灰岩が斜面に点々と散らばり、頂からは周防灘を一望する。
898.description_ja_short: 平尾台に立つ貫山地の主峰。羊群原の白い岩と周防灘の展望。
898.description_en_long: The 712 m main peak of the Nuki Mountains, standing at the northern end of Hiraodai, one of Japan's three great karst plateaus. White limestone outcrops dot its slopes like flocks of grazing sheep, and the summit surveys the whole of the Suo Sea.
898.description_en_short: Chief peak above the Hiraodai karst, with sheep-like white limestone and views over the Suo Sea.

899.description_ja_long: 宮城県気仙沼市に立つツツジの名山。初夏には約50万本のヤマツツジとレンゲツツジが山を真紅に染め、その彩りは遠い平野からも見えるという。標高710m。
899.description_ja_short: 初夏に50万本のツツジが山を真紅に染める気仙沼の名山。
899.description_en_long: A mountain in Kesennuma, Miyagi Prefecture, famed for its azaleas. In early summer some 500,000 yamatsutsuji and rengetsutsuji azaleas dye the 710 m mountain crimson, a blaze of color said to be visible even from the distant plains.
899.description_en_short: A Kesennuma mountain dyed crimson each early summer by some 500,000 azaleas.

900.description_ja_long: 愛媛県大洲の肱川流域に立つ独立峰で、東西二峰の高い方を女神南と呼ぶ。古来神の宿る山と崇められ、山裾にはメンヒルなど巨石遺跡が数多く残る。標高710m。
900.description_ja_short: 神の宿る山と崇められた独立峰。麓に巨石遺跡が点在する。
900.description_en_long: A 710 m independent peak in the Hijikawa river basin of Ozu, Ehime Prefecture, whose higher of two summits, east and west, is called Megannan. Revered since antiquity as a mountain where gods dwell, it keeps many megalithic remains, menhirs among them, along its skirts.
900.description_en_short: An independent peak revered as a dwelling of the gods, with megalithic remains dotting its foot.

901.description_ja_long: 筑波山に次ぐ筑波連山第二の高峰で標高709m。頂に加波山神社本宮を戴く信仰の山で、今も修験の禅定が行われる。明治には加波山事件の蜂起の舞台ともなった。
901.description_ja_short: 筑波連山第二の高峰。神を祀り修験が息づく信仰の山。
901.description_en_long: The second-highest peak of the Tsukuba range after Mt. Tsukuba, at 709 m. A mountain of faith crowned by the main sanctuary of Kabasan Shrine, it still hosts shugendo pilgrimage rites, and in the Meiji era it became the stage of the Kaba Incident uprising.
901.description_en_short: Second peak of the Tsukuba range, a mountain of faith where shugendo practice lives on.

902.description_ja_long: 福島県いわき市に立つ標高706mの山。昔、磐城・磐前・上遠野の明神が頂に集い郡の境を相談したという伝えが山名の由来で、春にはスミレが頂を彩る。
902.description_ja_short: 三柱の明神が頂に集い郡境を相談したという伝承を持つ山。
902.description_en_long: A 706 m mountain in Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture. Its name comes from a legend that the myojin deities of Iwaki, Iwasaki, and Kadono once gathered on the summit to confer over the district boundaries, and in spring violets adorn the mountaintop.
902.description_en_short: Named for a legend of three deities meeting on its summit to settle the district borders.

903.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県、大隅諸島の硫黄島にそびえる標高704mの山。島の名をそのまま戴いた峰で、海に囲まれた島の山として「しま山100選」にも選ばれている。
903.description_ja_short: 大隅諸島・硫黄島の山。しま山100選に数えられる島の峰。
903.description_en_long: A 704 m mountain rising on Iojima in the Osumi Islands of Kagoshima Prefecture. The peak bears the very name of its island, and as the mountain of a sea-girt isle it has been chosen as one of the 100 Island Mountains of Japan.
903.description_en_short: The peak of Iojima in the Osumi Islands, counted among the 100 Island Mountains.

904.description_ja_long: 北海道北部、中頓別町に位置する標高703mの山。高さこそ控えめだが三角錐の整った山容が美しく、頂に二等三角点を置き、眺望のよさでも知られている。
904.description_ja_short: 三角錐の整った姿が美しい道北の山。頂からの眺望もよい。
904.description_en_long: Pinneshiridake is a 703 m mountain in Nakatonbetsu, northern Hokkaido. Modest in height, it is admired for its shapely triangular pyramid form, holds a second-order triangulation point on its summit, and is known for its fine views.
904.description_en_short: A northern Hokkaido mountain with a beautifully shaped pyramid form and fine summit views.

905.description_ja_long: 神戸の街を見下ろす六甲山地の一峰で標高702m。山上の忉利天上寺が釈迦の生母・摩耶夫人を祀ることが名の由来。頂の掬星台から望む夜景は千万ドルと讃えられる。
905.description_ja_short: 釈迦の母、摩耶夫人を祀る六甲の峰。夜景の名所で知られる。
905.description_en_long: Mt. Maya is a 702 m peak of the Rokko Range overlooking Kobe. It is named for Toritenjo-ji Temple on the mountain, which enshrines Lady Maya, the birth mother of the Buddha. The night view from Kikuseidai on the summit is praised as a ten-million-dollar panorama.
905.description_en_short: A Rokko peak enshrining Lady Maya, the Buddha's mother, and famed for its night views.

906.description_ja_long: 秋吉台の北にそびえる標高702mの山。頂には北極星を仏に見立てた北辰妙見が祀られる。秋吉台を一望し、振り返れば日本海も光る絶好の展望の山。
906.description_ja_short: 秋吉台を見晴らす展望の頂に、北辰妙見を祀る信仰の山。
906.description_en_long: Katsuragisan rises to 702 m north of the Akiyoshidai plateau. Its summit enshrines Hokushin Myoken, a deity identifying the North Star with the Buddha, and commands a superb view over Akiyoshidai, with the Sea of Japan glinting behind.
906.description_en_short: A mountain of faith enshrining Hokushin Myoken on a summit that overlooks Akiyoshidai.

907.description_ja_long: 岡山県吉備中央町の最高峰で標高698m。一帯は郷土自然保護地域に指定された緑深い山で、頂からは遠く大山や瀬戸内海までも見渡すことができる。
907.description_ja_short: 吉備中央町の最高峰。遠く大山から瀬戸内海までを見渡す。
907.description_en_long: Ohirayama is the highest peak of Kibichuo in Okayama Prefecture at 698 m. The deeply green surroundings are designated a local nature conservation area, and from the summit the view stretches as far as distant Mt. Daisen and the Seto Inland Sea.
907.description_en_short: The highest peak of Kibichuo, with views reaching from distant Mt. Daisen to the Seto Inland Sea.

908.description_ja_long: 愛知県奥三河の霊山で標高695m。中腹の鳳来寺は飛鳥時代に利修仙人が開いたと伝わる古刹で、仏法僧と鳴くコノハズクの声が響く山としても名を馳せた。
908.description_ja_short: 利修仙人が開いたと伝わる古刹・鳳来寺を抱く奥三河の霊山。
908.description_en_long: Horaijisan is a 695 m sacred mountain in the Okumikawa region of Aichi. Horai-ji, the old temple on its slopes, is said to have been founded by the hermit Rishu in the Asuka period, and the mountain was also famed for the 'bupposo' calls of scops owls echoing there.
908.description_en_short: A sacred Okumikawa mountain holding Horai-ji, an old temple said to have been founded by the hermit Rishu.

909.description_ja_long: 奄美大島の最高峰で標高694m。島の開祖アマミコらが降り立ったと伝わる霊山で、雨に育まれた森は固有種の宝庫として頂一帯が国の天然記念物となっている。
909.description_ja_short: 島の開祖が降り立ったと伝わる奄美大島の最高峰。固有種の宝庫。
909.description_en_long: Yuwandake, 694 m, is the highest peak of Amami Oshima. A sacred mountain where Amamiko and the island's founding deities are said to have descended, its rain-nourished forest is a treasury of endemic species, and the summit area is a national natural monument.
909.description_en_short: The highest peak of Amami Oshima, where the island's founders are said to have descended; a treasury of endemic species.

910.description_ja_long: 福井・京都の境に双耳の峰を並べる標高693mの山。海側から仰ぐ姿の秀麗さで若狭富士と呼ばれる。泰澄が開いたと伝わる修験の山で、固有の花オオキンレイカが咲く。
910.description_ja_short: 若狭富士と呼ばれる双耳峰。修験の歴史と固有の花を持つ。
910.description_en_long: Aobayama raises twin peaks to 693 m on the border of Fukui and Kyoto. Its graceful form seen from the sea earned it the name Wakasa Fuji. A mountain of Shugendo practice said to have been opened by Taicho, it blooms with the endemic flower ookinreika.
910.description_en_short: A twin-peaked mountain called Wakasa Fuji, with a Shugendo history and an endemic flower.

911.description_ja_long: 山口県下関市と長門市の境にそびえる標高691mの山で、長門市の最高峰。頂には一等三角点が据えられ、天井の名にふさわしく一帯の山並みの高みをなしている。
911.description_ja_short: 長門市の最高峰。頂に一等三角点を据える県西部の山。
911.description_en_long: Tenjogadake rises to 691 m on the border of Shimonoseki and Nagato in Yamaguchi Prefecture, the highest peak of Nagato. A first-order triangulation point sits on its summit, and true to its name, meaning 'ceiling,' it forms the high point of the surrounding ranges.
911.description_en_short: The highest peak of Nagato, a mountain in western Yamaguchi with a first-order triangulation point on top.

912.description_ja_long: 熊本・鹿児島県境に立つ三角錐の鋭峰で標高687m。八代海から望むと矢筈の形を見せ、古くから海上交通の目印とされた。頂の巨石には高山彦九郎の歌が刻まれる。
912.description_ja_short: 矢筈の形を海に見せる県境の鋭峰。海の道標とされた山。
912.description_en_long: Yahazudake is a sharp 687 m pyramidal peak on the Kumamoto-Kagoshima border. Seen from the Yatsushiro Sea it shows the notched shape of an arrow nock and long served as a landmark for sea traffic. A poem by Takayama Hikokuro is carved on a huge rock at the summit.
912.description_en_short: A sharp border peak showing an arrow-nock shape to the sea, long used as a mariners' landmark.

913.description_ja_long: 丹後半島のほぼ中央に位置する標高683mの山で、半島有数の高峰。江戸期の地誌には太鼓ケ山と記された。頂に一等三角点を置き、南と西へ展望が開けている。
913.description_ja_short: 丹後半島の中央に立つ高峰。頂に一等三角点が据わる。
913.description_en_long: Taikoyama stands near the center of the Tango Peninsula at 683 m, one of the peninsula's loftiest peaks. Edo-period gazetteers recorded it as Taikogayama. A first-order triangulation point rests on the summit, with views opening to the south and west.
913.description_en_short: A lofty peak at the center of the Tango Peninsula, with a first-order triangulation point on its summit.

914.description_ja_long: 熊本県天草上島にそびえる天草諸島の最高峰。山頂には航海の安全を祈って祀られた倉岳神社が鎮座し、八代海の向こうに雲仙や九州の山々を見渡す。標高682m。
914.description_ja_short: 天草の最高峰。山頂の倉岳神社に航海の安全が祈られた。
914.description_en_long: Kuradake, rising on Kamishima island in Kumamoto, is the highest peak of the Amakusa Islands at 682 m. Kuradake Shrine on the summit was founded with prayers for safety at sea, and the view sweeps across the Yatsushiro Sea to Unzen and the mountains of Kyushu.
914.description_en_short: The highest peak of Amakusa; Kuradake Shrine on its summit received prayers for safe voyages.

915.description_ja_long: 広島市中心部の東にそびえ、市街からもよく目立つ山。麓には水と親しめる水分峡森林公園が広がり、アカマツ林の尾根はハイカーに親しまれている。標高682m。
915.description_ja_short: 広島市街の東に目立つ山。麓の水分峡森林公園は憩いの場。
915.description_en_long: Gosasozan rises east of central Hiroshima and stands out well from the city streets, reaching 682 m. Mikumari Gorge Forest Park, where visitors can enjoy the water, spreads at its foot, and its red-pine ridges are well loved by hikers.
915.description_en_short: A prominent mountain east of central Hiroshima; Mikumari Gorge Forest Park at its foot is a place of rest.

916.description_ja_long: 宇治茶の里、和束町と宇治田原町にまたがる山。「北大峯」と呼ばれた金胎寺が山上に堂舎を構え、鎌倉時代の宝篋印塔が長い信仰の歳月を物語る。標高682m。
916.description_ja_short: 茶どころに立つ信仰の山。北大峯と呼ばれた金胎寺を戴く。
916.description_en_long: Jubusen straddles Wazuka and Ujitawara, home of Uji tea. Kontai-ji Temple, once called the 'Northern Omine,' keeps its halls on the mountain, and a Kamakura-period hokyointo pagoda tells of the long centuries of faith here. It stands 682 m high.
916.description_en_short: A mountain of faith in tea country, crowned by Kontai-ji Temple, once called the Northern Omine.

917.description_ja_long: 京都と大阪の境、老ノ坂山地の最高峰。山頂で足踏みするとポンポンと音がすることが名の由来と伝わるが定かでなく、古くは加茂勢山と呼ばれた。標高679m。
917.description_ja_short: 山頂の足踏みの音が名になったと伝わる京都西山の最高峰。
917.description_en_long: Ponponyama is the highest peak of the Oinosaka range on the border of Kyoto and Osaka, at 679 m. Its name is said to come from a 'pon-pon' sound heard when stamping on the summit, though this is uncertain, and in older times it was called Kamoseyama.
917.description_en_short: The highest peak of Kyoto's western hills, said to be named for the sound of stamping on its summit.

918.description_ja_long: 鹿児島市と薩摩川内市の境になだらかな稜線を横たえる山。尾根筋には銭を積み上げたような姿の奇岩「銭積岩」が座り、登る人の目を楽しませる。標高677m。
918.description_ja_short: 穏やかな山容の頂。銭を積んだような奇岩・銭積岩が座る。
918.description_en_long: Yaeyama lays a gentle ridgeline along the border of Kagoshima City and Satsumasendai. On the ridge sits Zenitsumi-iwa, a curious rock that looks like a stack of piled-up coins, delighting the eyes of those who climb. It stands 677 m high.
918.description_en_short: A gently shaped summit where Zenitsumi-iwa, a curious rock like stacked coins, rests on the ridge.

919.description_ja_long: 津軽半島の山並みにあって、全山をブナ林が深く覆う。その自然は学術的にも貴重とされ、県の自然環境保全地域に指定されて静かに守られている。標高670m。
919.description_ja_short: ブナ林に包まれた津軽半島の山。県の保全地域に指定。
919.description_en_long: Yotsudakiyama stands among the mountains of the Tsugaru Peninsula, its entire slopes deeply cloaked in beech forest. This nature is considered academically valuable, and the mountain is quietly protected as a prefectural nature conservation area. It rises 670 m.
919.description_en_short: A Tsugaru Peninsula mountain wrapped in beech forest, protected as a prefectural conservation area.

920.description_ja_long: 長門市と美祢市の境、日本海と瀬戸内海の分水界に立つ展望の山。山頂の権現祠の石像は盗難に遭ったが、祟りを恐れて戻されたという逸話が残る。標高669m。
920.description_ja_short: 二つの海の分水界に立つ展望峰。祠の石像の逸話が残る。
920.description_en_long: Hanaoyama stands on the border of Nagato and Mine, on the watershed between the Sea of Japan and the Seto Inland Sea, offering fine views. A stone image at the summit's Gongen shrine was once stolen but, the story goes, was returned for fear of a curse. It rises 669 m.
920.description_en_short: A scenic peak on the divide between two seas, with a curious tale about its shrine's stone image.

921.description_ja_long: 北海道南部、渡島半島の山なみに頭をもたげる一峰。山頂には一等三角点が据えられ、さえぎるもののない眺めが全方位に広がる展望の頂である。標高665m。
921.description_ja_short: 一等三角点を戴く渡島半島の山。全方位の展望が広がる。
921.description_en_long: Hachimandake lifts its head among the mountains of the Oshima Peninsula in southern Hokkaido. A first-order triangulation point is set on the summit, a viewpoint where unobstructed panoramas spread in every direction. The peak stands 665 m high.
921.description_en_short: An Oshima Peninsula mountain crowned with a first-order triangulation point and panoramic views in all directions.

922.description_ja_long: 熊本市の西に三角錐の姿を見せる複式火山の主峰。平安期に大和の蔵王権現を勧請して金峰山と称し、麓には漱石『草枕』ゆかりの峠の茶屋跡も残る。標高665m。
922.description_ja_short: 熊本市民に親しまれる三角錐の山。『草枕』の舞台にも近い。
922.description_en_long: Kinbozan shows a triangular pyramid west of Kumamoto City, the main peak of a compound volcano. It was named in the Heian period when the Zao Gongen of Yamato was enshrined here, and the site of the pass teahouse linked to Soseki's Kusamakura remains at its foot. 665 m.
922.description_en_short: A pyramidal mountain beloved by the people of Kumamoto, close to the setting of Kusamakura.

923.description_ja_long: 広島県三原市の背後に横たわり、市内で最も高い頂をもつ山。龍王の名を戴く峰は各地にあるが、この山もまた市域を足元に静かに見下ろしている。標高665m。
923.description_ja_short: 三原市の最高峰。龍王の名を戴き、市街を静かに見下ろす。
923.description_en_long: Ryuosan stretches behind the city of Mihara in Hiroshima Prefecture and holds the highest summit within the city. Peaks bearing the Dragon King's name are found across Japan, and this one too quietly looks down over the city spread at its feet. It rises 665 m.
923.description_en_short: The highest peak of Mihara, bearing the Dragon King's name and quietly overlooking the city.

924.description_ja_long: 丹後半島の付け根にどっしりと構える山。天女が真名井の池に舞い降りたという日本最古とされる羽衣伝説の地で、山頂から天橋立を遠く見下ろす。標高661m。
924.description_ja_short: 日本最古とされる羽衣伝説の山。頂から天橋立を望む。
924.description_en_long: Isanagosan sits solidly at the base of the Tango Peninsula. It is the setting of what is held to be Japan's oldest hagoromo legend, in which a celestial maiden descended to the Manai pond, and the summit looks far down on Amanohashidate. It stands 661 m high.
924.description_en_short: The mountain of Japan's oldest celestial-robe legend, with a summit view of Amanohashidate.

925.description_ja_long: 落語「妙見参り」にも登場する能勢の霊山。山上の妙見堂は日蓮宗の西の拠点として参詣者を集め、山頂一帯には自生南限とされるブナ林が茂る。標高660m。
925.description_ja_short: 能勢妙見堂を戴く北摂の信仰の山。貴重なブナ林も茂る。
925.description_en_long: Myokensan is a sacred mountain of Nose appearing in the rakugo tale 'Myoken Mairi.' The Myokendo hall on its top drew pilgrims as a western center of the Nichiren sect, and a beech forest held to be at the southern limit of natural growth covers the summit area. 660 m.
925.description_en_short: A mountain of faith in northern Osaka crowned by Nose Myokendo, with a precious beech forest.

926.description_ja_long: 高知県南西部の山。頂から五つの在所を望めたことが名の由来と伝わる展望の頂で、大師伝説も残り、山中には戦時中の哨戒所跡が静かに眠る。標高658m。
926.description_ja_short: 五つの在所を見渡す名をもつ山。日の出の美しさで知られる。
926.description_en_long: Gozaishonomine is a mountain in southwestern Kochi. Its name is said to come from the five hamlets once visible from the top, a summit of fine views. Legends of Kobo Daishi also remain, and the ruins of a wartime lookout post rest quietly on the mountain. 658 m.
926.description_en_short: A mountain named for the five hamlets seen from its top, known for the beauty of its sunrises.

927.description_ja_long: 茨城県北部、巨石信仰の面影を残す霊山。山頂近くの太刀割石は直径7mの巨岩が刃物で断ったように割れ、源義家が太刀で割ったという伝説を宿す。標高658m。
927.description_ja_short: 巨石信仰の霊山。源義家伝説の太刀割石が頂近くに眠る。
927.description_en_long: Tatsuwaresan is a sacred mountain in northern Ibaraki that keeps traces of megalith worship. Near the summit, the Tachiwari-ishi, a huge rock 7 m across, is split as if cut by a blade, and legend holds that Minamoto no Yoshiie cleaved it with his sword. It rises 658 m.
927.description_en_short: A sacred mountain of megalith worship; the Tachiwari-ishi of the Yoshiie legend rests near its top.

928.description_ja_long: 屋久島の西方沖に浮かぶ火山島、口永良部島の山。北隣の新岳は今も活動を続けており、火山とともに生きる島の歴史を静かに見つめてきた頂である。標高657m。
928.description_ja_short: 口永良部島にそびえる火山の頂。隣の新岳は今も活動する。
928.description_en_long: Furudake is a mountain on Kuchinoerabujima, a volcanic island floating off the western coast of Yakushima. Its northern neighbor Shindake remains active today, and this summit has quietly watched over the island's history of living with its volcanoes. It rises 657 m.
928.description_en_short: A volcanic summit rising on Kuchinoerabujima; neighboring Shindake is still active today.

929.description_ja_long: 福井市の西、丹生山地に大きな山体を横たえる。国中を見下ろすゆえに名づくと古書に記され、一等三角点の頂からは越前の山河と日本海を一望する。標高656m。
929.description_ja_short: 国中を見下ろす名の由来をもつ山。越前の展望が広がる。
929.description_en_long: Kunimidake spreads its broad mass across the Nyu Mountains west of Fukui City. Old records say it was named for looking down over the whole province, and its 656 m summit, with a first-order triangulation point, surveys the hills of Echizen and the Sea of Japan.
929.description_en_short: A mountain said to be named for looking down over the whole province, with sweeping views of Echizen.

930.description_ja_long: 奥久慈の中心にそびえる標高654mの岩峰。西面と南面は絶壁となって切れ落ち、その鋭い姿は文人大町桂月が絵を学ばんと詠んだほどの威容である。
930.description_ja_short: 絶壁を巡らす奥久慈の岩峰。大町桂月が詠んだ鋭い山容。
930.description_en_long: Nantaisan is a 654 m rocky peak rising at the heart of the Okukuji region. Its west and south faces drop away in sheer cliffs, and its sharp profile is so imposing that the man of letters Omachi Keigetsu wrote of wishing to learn painting from it.
930.description_en_short: A rocky Okukuji peak ringed by sheer cliffs, its sharp form celebrated in verse by Omachi Keigetsu.

932.description_ja_long: 国境の島・対馬の最高峰。山頂には三等三角点が据えられ、晴れた日には海の彼方に朝鮮半島が浮かぶという、大陸への近さを実感させる頂である。標高648m。
932.description_ja_short: 対馬の最高峰。晴れれば海の向こうに朝鮮半島が浮かぶ。
932.description_en_long: Yatateyama is the highest peak of Tsushima, Japan's border island. A third-order triangulation point sits on its 648 m summit, and on clear days the Korean Peninsula is said to appear across the sea, a summit that brings home how close the continent lies.
932.description_en_short: The highest peak of Tsushima; on clear days the Korean Peninsula floats into view across the sea.

933.description_ja_long: 佐渡島の南側に連なる小佐渡山地の最高峰。島の背骨をなす山なみの中でひときわ高く、日本海に浮かぶ島の暮らしを見守り続けてきた頂である。標高646m。
933.description_ja_short: 小佐渡山地の最高峰。佐渡島の南の山なみにそびえる。
933.description_en_long: Ojiyama is the highest peak of the Kosado Mountains, which run along the southern side of Sado Island. Standing tallest in the range that forms the island's backbone, this 646 m summit has long watched over life on the island in the Sea of Japan.
933.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Kosado Mountains, rising over the southern ranges of Sado Island.

934.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県のはるか南、奄美諸島の徳之島にそびえる山。島の高みとして海原を見下ろし、島の暮らしとともにあり続けてきた素朴な頂である。標高645m。
934.description_ja_short: 奄美諸島・徳之島にそびえる頂。島の高みから海原を望む。
934.description_en_long: Inokawadake rises on Tokunoshima in the Amami Islands, far to the south of mainland Kagoshima. As one of the island's high points, this unassuming 645 m summit looks down over the surrounding ocean and has long stood close beside island life.
934.description_en_short: A peak on Tokunoshima in the Amami Islands, gazing over the ocean from the island's heights.

935.description_ja_long: 大阪と奈良を分かつ生駒山地の主峰。神武東征の戦いが日本書紀に記され、役行者の鬼退治伝説も宿す古い山だが、今は山上に遊園地が広がる。標高642m。
935.description_ja_short: 大阪と奈良を分かつ主峰。神話と遊園地が同居する山。
935.description_en_long: Mt. Ikoma is the main peak of the Ikoma Range dividing Osaka from Nara. The Nihon Shoki records a battle of Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition here, and legend tells of En no Gyoja subduing demons, yet today an amusement park spreads across the 642 m summit.
935.description_en_short: The main peak dividing Osaka and Nara, where ancient myth and an amusement park share the mountain.

936.description_ja_long: 能登半島の最高峰。名は江戸期まで採掘された金山に由来するとされ、山頂には手速比咩神社の奥宮が鎮座。ブナ林の彼方に白山や立山を望む。標高637m。
936.description_ja_short: 金山に名を由来する能登半島の最高峰。白山や立山を望む。
936.description_en_long: Hodatsusan is the highest peak of the Noto Peninsula at 637 m. Its name is said to derive from a gold mine worked until the Edo period, and the inner sanctuary of Tehayahime Shrine stands on the summit, where views reach past beech forest to Hakusan and Tateyama.
936.description_en_short: The Noto Peninsula's highest peak, named after a gold mine, with views of Hakusan and Tateyama.

938.description_ja_long: 越後平野の縁に日本海からせり上がる秀峰。全山が越後国の総鎮守・弥彦神社の神域とされ、英彦山、雪彦山とともに日本三彦山に数えられる。標高634m。
938.description_ja_short: 弥彦神社の神体山。日本三彦山に数えられる越後の霊峰。
938.description_en_long: Mt. Yahiko is a graceful 634 m peak rising from the Sea of Japan at the edge of the Echigo Plain. The whole mountain is the sacred precinct of Yahiko Shrine, guardian of old Echigo Province, and with Hikosan and Seppikosan it is one of Japan's Three Hiko Mountains.
938.description_en_short: The sacred body of Yahiko Shrine and a holy peak of Echigo, counted among Japan's Three Hiko Mountains.

940.description_ja_long: 山口県防府市の背後にそびえ、山頂からは屈指の眺めが広がる。頂上の一帯は公園として整えられ、つつじ園や芝生の広場が憩いの場となっている。標高631m。
940.description_ja_short: 防府市街を見下ろす展望の山。頂にはつつじ園が広がる。
940.description_en_long: Ohirayama rises behind the city of Hofu in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and its 631 m summit commands one of the finest views in the region. The summit area is laid out as a park, where an azalea garden and grassy lawns offer visitors a place to relax.
940.description_en_short: A viewpoint mountain overlooking the city of Hofu, with an azalea garden spreading across its summit.

941.description_ja_long: 豊田市と瀬戸市の境に秀麗な姿を見せる霊山。名は景行天皇が伊勢行幸の折に猿を海へ投げた伝説に由来するといい、麓に猿投神社が鎮座する。標高629m。
941.description_ja_short: 猿投げ伝説に名を由来する霊山。麓に猿投神社が鎮座。
941.description_en_long: Sanageyama shows a graceful figure on the border of Toyota and Seto. This sacred 629 m mountain is said to take its name from a legend that Emperor Keiko threw a monkey into the sea during a journey to Ise, and Sanage Shrine stands at its foot.
941.description_en_short: A sacred mountain named for the legend of a thrown monkey, with Sanage Shrine at its foot.

942.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県のトカラ列島、口之島に立つ標高628mの山。海を隔てて島々が点々と連なる列島にあって、島の空へ頭をもたげる一座である。
942.description_ja_short: 鹿児島・トカラ列島の口之島にそびえる標高628mの離島の山。
942.description_en_long: Maedake is a 628 m mountain standing on Kuchinoshima in the Tokara Islands of Kagoshima Prefecture. In this chain where small isles stretch one after another across the open sea, it is the peak that lifts its head into the island sky.
942.description_en_short: A 628 m mountain rising on Kuchinoshima, a remote isle in Kagoshima's Tokara Islands.

943.description_ja_long: 茨城県日立市と常陸太田市の境、多賀山地の南部に横たわるなだらかな山。花の百名山に数えられ、センブリが代表の花。山頂からは筑波山や那須連山まで見渡せる。
943.description_ja_short: 多賀山地南部のなだらかな主峰。センブリ咲く花の百名山。
943.description_en_long: Takasuzuyama is a gentle mountain lying in the southern Taga Mountains, on the border of Hitachi and Hitachiota in Ibaraki. Counted among the 100 Famous Flower Mountains, it is known above all for swertia blossoms, and summit views reach Mt. Tsukuba and the Nasu Range.
943.description_en_short: A gentle peak of the southern Taga Mountains, a famous flower mountain known for swertia blossoms.

944.description_ja_long: 薩摩半島の南約60km、洋上に浮かぶ離島・黒島にそびえる標高620mの山。島は「ミニ屋久島」とも呼ばれるほど自然が濃く、山も深い緑に包まれる。
944.description_ja_short: 「ミニ屋久島」と呼ばれる黒島の山。緑豊かな離島の頂。
944.description_en_long: Yaguradake is a 620 m mountain on Kuroshima, a remote island in the open sea about 60 km south of the Satsuma Peninsula. The island's nature is so rich that it is called a miniature Yakushima, and the mountain itself is wrapped in deep green.
944.description_en_short: A mountain on Kuroshima, a lushly green remote island known as a miniature Yakushima.

945.description_ja_long: 若狭湾に突き出す内外海半島の主峰で標高619m。かつて小浜へ出入りする北前船の目印とされた。北面は海食崖が連なり、海食洞・蘇洞門の奇観を海上に刻む。
945.description_ja_short: 北前船の目印とされた内外海半島の主峰。蘇洞門を刻む。
945.description_en_long: Kusuyagadake, 619 m, is the main peak of the Uchitomi Peninsula jutting into Wakasa Bay. It once served as a landmark for kitamaebune trading ships calling at Obama. Sea cliffs line its northern face, carving the strange seascape of the Sotomo sea caves.
945.description_en_short: The main peak of the Uchitomi Peninsula, once a seamark for trading ships, with the Sotomo sea caves below.

946.description_ja_long: 徳島県阿南市と那賀町の境に立つ標高618mの山。山頂近くに四国霊場二十一番札所・太龍寺が構え、若き弘法大師が唐へ渡る前に修行したと伝わる信仰の山。
946.description_ja_short: 太龍寺を頂に抱く信仰の山。弘法大師修行の地と伝わる。
946.description_en_long: Tairyujiyama stands at 618 m on the border of Anan and Naka in Tokushima Prefecture. Near the summit sits Tairyuji, temple 21 of the Shikoku pilgrimage, and this mountain of faith is where the young Kobo Daishi is said to have trained before crossing to Tang China.
946.description_en_short: A mountain of faith crowned by Tairyuji temple, where Kobo Daishi is said to have trained.

947.description_ja_long: 渡島半島の東端、海に突き出す活火山で標高618m。山名はアイヌ語に由来し「溶岩が出る」の意とも「岬」の意ともいう。ツツジ咲く頃の山肌の美しさで知られる。
947.description_ja_short: 海に突き出す渡島半島の活火山。ツツジの群落で名高い。
947.description_en_long: Mt. Esan is a 618 m active volcano thrusting into the sea at the eastern tip of the Oshima Peninsula. Its name derives from Ainu, meaning either 'where lava flows out' or 'cape,' and the mountain is known for the beauty of its slopes when azaleas bloom.
947.description_en_short: An active volcano jutting into the sea from the Oshima Peninsula, famed for its azalea colonies.

948.description_ja_long: 山口県下関市にある弘法大師信仰の山で標高616m。狗留孫とは梵語の音訳で「実に妙なる成就」を意味するという。中腹の修禅寺は御嶽観音として広く崇敬を集める。
948.description_ja_short: 梵語に由来する名を持つ弘法大師信仰の山。修禅寺が建つ。
948.description_en_long: Kurusonzan (Ontake) is a 616 m mountain of Kobo Daishi worship in Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Kuruson is said to transliterate a Sanskrit phrase meaning 'truly wondrous attainment,' and Shuzenji temple on its slopes is widely revered as the Ontake Kannon.
948.description_en_short: A mountain of Kobo Daishi worship with a Sanskrit-derived name, home to Shuzenji temple.

949.description_ja_long: 香川県の西部、瀬戸内海を間近にする標高616mの山。琴平街道から仰いだ山容が象の頭を思わせることからこの名が付いたと伝わり、県のみどり百選にも選ばれた。
949.description_ja_short: 琴平街道から見た姿が象の頭に似ることに名を由来する山。
949.description_en_long: Zozusan is a 616 m mountain in western Kagawa Prefecture, close to the Seto Inland Sea. Its name is said to come from its profile as seen from the Kotohira highway, which suggests an elephant's head, and it was chosen among the prefecture's 100 best green spots.
949.description_en_short: A mountain named for its elephant's-head profile as seen from the Kotohira highway.

950.description_ja_long: 青森県南部、南部町と三戸町にまたがる標高615mの山。均整のとれた姿は独立峰のように望まれる。古くから霊峰と仰がれ、麓に法光寺などの古刹が立ち並ぶ。
950.description_ja_short: 均整のとれた姿の霊峰。麓に法光寺などの寺が連なる。
950.description_en_long: Nakuidake is a 615 m mountain straddling the towns of Nanbu and Sannohe in southern Aomori. Its well-balanced form makes it appear like a freestanding peak. Long revered as a sacred mountain, it has old temples such as Hokoji standing in rows at its foot.
950.description_en_short: A sacred peak of well-balanced form, with old temples such as Hokoji lining its foot.

951.description_ja_long: 下関市、鬼ガ城山地の南端に位置する山。竜は水を司る神で、麓に竜王神社が祭られ、雨乞いが行われてきたと伝わる。頂からは響灘や九州、瀬戸内海までも見渡せる。
951.description_ja_short: 響灘と瀬戸内を見渡す展望の山。雨乞いの信仰を伝える。
951.description_en_long: Ryuozan stands at the southern end of the Onigajo Mountains in Shimonoseki. The dragon is a deity of water, and Ryuo Shrine at the foot long hosted prayers for rain. From the summit the view sweeps over the Hibiki Sea, Kyushu, and even the Seto Inland Sea.
951.description_en_short: A viewpoint over the Hibiki Sea and Seto Inland Sea, keeping alive a tradition of rainmaking rites.

952.description_ja_long: 長崎・佐賀の県境に鋭くそびえる標高609mの峰。川棚側から望む姿はマッターホルンにも例えられる。山名は頂の形が虚空蔵菩薩の宝冠に似ることによるという。
952.description_ja_short: マッターホルンに例えられる鋭峰。頂に虚空蔵菩薩を祭る。
952.description_en_long: Kokuzosan is a sharp 609 m peak rising on the border of Nagasaki and Saga. Seen from the Kawatana side it has been likened to the Matterhorn, and the mountain's name is said to come from the summit's resemblance to the jeweled crown of Kokuzo Bosatsu.
952.description_en_short: A sharp peak likened to the Matterhorn, with Kokuzo Bosatsu enshrined on its summit.

953.description_ja_long: 島根県沖に浮かぶ隠岐諸島の最高峰で標高608m。山名は中腹の寺に由来し、古くは近海を行く船の目印とされた。オキシャクナゲの群生や杉の原生林を宿す。
953.description_ja_short: 船の目印とされた隠岐の最高峰。シャクナゲと杉原生林の山。
953.description_en_long: Daimanjisan is the highest peak of the Oki Islands off Shimane Prefecture, at 608 m. Named after a temple on its slopes, it long served as a landmark for ships sailing nearby waters, and it shelters colonies of Oki rhododendron and a primeval cedar forest.
953.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Oki Islands, once a seamark, with rhododendrons and primeval cedars.

954.description_ja_long: 淡路島の南部に横たわる島の最高峰で標高608m。珍しい山名は樹木のユズリハに由来するという。柏原山・先山とともに淡路三山と呼ばれ、古くから信仰を集めた。
954.description_ja_short: ユズリハに名を由来する淡路島の最高峰。淡路三山の一峰。
954.description_en_long: Yuzuruhasan, 608 m, lies across southern Awaji Island as the island's highest peak. Its unusual name is said to derive from the yuzuriha tree. Together with Kashiwarayama and Senzan it is called one of the Three Mountains of Awaji, long an object of worship.
954.description_en_short: Awaji Island's highest peak, named after the yuzuriha tree and one of the Three Mountains of Awaji.

955.description_ja_long: 九州の西の洋上に連なる甑島列島、その最高峰として下甑島にそびえる標高604mの山。頂には一等三角点が置かれ、しま山100選にも数えられる。
955.description_ja_short: 下甑島にそびえる甑島列島の最高峰。一等三角点の山。
955.description_en_long: Otake rises on Shimokoshikijima as the highest peak of the Koshikijima Islands, which stretch across the sea west of Kyushu. A first-order triangulation point is set on its 604 m summit, and it is also counted among the 100 Island Mountains of Japan.
955.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Koshikijima Islands, rising on Shimokoshikijima with a first-order triangulation point.

956.description_ja_long: 東京都八王子市に立つ標高599mの山。奈良時代の高僧行基の開山と伝わる薬王院を擁し、天狗伝説を宿す修験の霊山。植物は1,600種を超え、四季の彩りが深い。
956.description_ja_short: 天狗伝説と薬王院で知られる修験の霊山。植物の宝庫。
956.description_en_long: Mt. Takao is a 599 m mountain in Hachioji, Tokyo. It holds Yakuoin temple, said to have been founded by the Nara-period priest Gyoki, and is a sacred Shugendo mountain steeped in tengu legend. With more than 1,600 plant species, its seasonal colors run deep.
956.description_en_short: A sacred Shugendo mountain known for tengu legends and Yakuoin temple, and a treasure house of plants.

957.description_ja_long: 北九州市の小倉の背後にそびえる山。配流の途中に足を痛めた和気清麻呂が麓の霊泉で癒え、「足が立った」ことが山名の由来と伝わる。霧が多く霧ヶ岳の別名を持つ。
957.description_ja_short: 和気清麻呂の伝説に名を由来する小倉の山。別名霧ヶ岳。
957.description_en_long: Rising behind Kokura in Kitakyushu, this mountain takes its name from a legend: the exiled Wake no Kiyomaro, his injured legs healed at a sacred spring at its foot, found he could stand again. Often wrapped in fog, it is also known as Kirigatake.
957.description_en_short: A mountain above Kokura named for the legend of Wake no Kiyomaro; also known as Kirigatake.

958.description_ja_long: 秋田県由利本荘市、赤田五峰と呼ばれる山塊の一峰で標高594m。麓の長谷寺は日本三大観音の一つといわれる観音像を祀り、開山の是山和尚が修行した山とされる。
958.description_ja_short: 赤田五峰の一峰。麓の長谷寺を開いた和尚の修行の地。
958.description_en_long: A 594 m peak in Yurihonjo, Akita, one of the Akada Five Peaks. Hasedera Temple at its foot enshrines a Kannon statue said to be one of Japan's three great Kannon, and the mountain is held to be where Zesan, the temple's founding priest, once trained.
958.description_en_short: One of the Akada Five Peaks, and the training ground of the priest who founded Hasedera Temple at its foot.

959.description_ja_long: 京都と滋賀の境に横たわる標高593mの山。北には逢坂関の旧跡が残る古来の往来の要地。中腹に牛尾観音と親しまれる法厳寺が建ち、頂は比叡山や愛宕山を望む。
959.description_ja_short: 逢坂関の歴史を伝える京滋境の山。中腹に牛尾観音が建つ。
959.description_en_long: A 593 m mountain lying on the border of Kyoto and Shiga, a key point on ancient routes with the remains of the Osaka Barrier to its north. Hogonji Temple, affectionately known as Ushio Kannon, stands on its slopes, and the summit looks out to Mt. Hiei and Mt. Atago.
959.description_en_short: A mountain on the Kyoto-Shiga border steeped in the history of the Osaka Barrier, with the Ushio Kannon temple on its slopes.

960.description_ja_long: 和歌山県の山中にひっそりと立つ標高591mの山。広く知られた名所ではないが、その頂には測量の基準となる一等三角点が据えられ、静かな存在感を放っている。
960.description_ja_short: 和歌山の山中に立つ一等三角点の峰。静かな存在感を放つ。
960.description_en_long: A 591 m mountain standing quietly deep in the hills of Wakayama Prefecture. Though not a widely known destination, its summit holds a first-order triangulation point that serves as a surveying benchmark, lending this modest peak a quiet presence.
960.description_en_short: A quiet peak in the Wakayama hills, crowned with a first-order triangulation point.

961.description_ja_long: 薩摩半島の南西端、野間半島に急峻な円錐形を描いて立つ山。開聞岳・金峰山と並ぶ南薩の三岳の一つで、古くは外国貿易の船路の目印とされた。中腹に野間神社を祭る。
961.description_ja_short: 開聞岳・金峰山と並び南薩三岳に数えられる急峻な円錐峰。
961.description_en_long: A steep conical peak on the Noma Peninsula at the southwestern tip of the Satsuma Peninsula. Counted with Kaimondake and Kinpozan among the three peaks of southern Satsuma, it once guided foreign trading ships as a landmark, and Noma Shrine stands partway up its slopes.
961.description_en_short: A steep conical peak counted with Kaimondake and Kinpozan among the three peaks of southern Satsuma.

962.description_ja_long: 長崎半島の背をなす長崎市の最高峰で標高590m。山名は九州で勢力を広げた鎮西八郎為朝にちなむと伝わる。草原の頂は展望が開け、半島の両側に海を見渡せる。
962.description_ja_short: 鎮西八郎為朝に名を由来する長崎市の最高峰。海望む頂。
962.description_en_long: At 590 m the highest peak in the city of Nagasaki, forming the spine of the Nagasaki Peninsula. The name is said to derive from Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo, a warrior who spread his power across Kyushu. Its grassy summit overlooks the sea on both sides of the peninsula.
962.description_en_short: Nagasaki City's highest peak, named after the warrior Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo, its summit overlooking the sea.

964.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県のトカラ列島、悪石島にそびえる標高584mの山。南海に島々が連なる列島にあって、しま山100選に数えられる離島の頂である。
964.description_ja_short: トカラ列島・悪石島の山。しま山100選の離島の頂。
964.description_en_long: A 584 m mountain rising on Akusekijima in the Tokara Islands of Kagoshima Prefecture. Set among this chain of islands strung across the southern seas, it stands as a remote island summit selected as one of the 100 Island Mountains of Japan.
964.description_en_short: The peak of Akusekijima in the Tokara Islands, an island summit chosen among the 100 Island Mountains.

965.description_ja_long: 福岡県宮若市、三郡山地の北に潜む山。低山ながら道は険しく、「犬でも悲鳴をあげて登った」ことが名の由来という。別名熊ヶ城といい、戦国の山城の史伝が残る。
965.description_ja_short: 「犬でも悲鳴をあげた」険しさに名を由来する山。別名熊ヶ城。
965.description_en_long: A mountain hidden north of the Sangun range in Miyawaka, Fukuoka. Though low, its paths are rugged, and the name is said to come from the saying that even a dog cried out climbing it. Its other name, Kumagajo, preserves the history of a Sengoku-period mountain castle.
965.description_en_short: A rugged Fukuoka mountain said to have made even a dog cry on the climb; also called Kumagajo.

966.description_ja_long: 北海道南西の日本海に浮かぶ離島・奥尻島にそびえる標高584mの山。島の最高峰として島影の頂点をなし、洋上はるかからもその高まりが望まれる。
966.description_ja_short: 日本海に浮かぶ奥尻島の最高峰。島影の頂点をなす山。
966.description_en_long: A 584 m mountain rising on Okushiri Island, a remote isle in the Sea of Japan off southwestern Hokkaido. As the island's highest peak it crowns the silhouette of Okushiri, and its swelling form can be made out from far across the water.
966.description_en_short: The highest peak of Okushiri Island in the Sea of Japan, crowning the island's silhouette.

967.description_ja_long: 福井県小浜市の南西に盛り上がる標高584mの山。青葉山、多田ヶ岳とともに若狭三山に数えられ、頂に立てば残る二山の姿を望むことができる。
967.description_ja_short: 青葉山・多田ヶ岳と若狭三山をなす山。頂から二山を望む。
967.description_en_long: A 584 m mountain swelling to the southwest of Obama in Fukui Prefecture. Together with Aobayama and Tadagadake it is counted among the Wakasa Sanzan, the three noted mountains of Wakasa, and from its summit the other two peaks can both be seen.
967.description_en_short: One of the Wakasa Sanzan together with Aobayama and Tadagadake, both visible from its summit.

969.description_ja_long: 伊豆諸島・神津島の最高峰で標高572m。承和五年の大噴火で溶岩ドームを築いた火山で、平坦な頂には池が点在し、砂の「表砂漠」「裏砂漠」が広がる。
969.description_ja_short: 神津島の最高峰。池と白い砂漠が点在する平坦な頂の火山。
969.description_en_long: The highest peak of Kozushima in the Izu Islands at 572 m. A volcano that built a lava dome in the great eruption of 838, it carries a flat summit area where ponds are scattered and sandy expanses known as the front and back deserts spread out.
969.description_en_short: Kozushima's highest peak, a volcano whose flat top holds scattered ponds and white sandy deserts.

970.description_ja_long: 城崎温泉の背後にそびえる兵庫県北部の山。読み方が土地ごとに異なるといい、一等三角点の頂には十四体の石仏が並び、雲海の湧く朝の景観は幻想的と評される。
970.description_ja_short: 城崎温泉を見下ろす山。石仏が並ぶ頂から雲海を望む。
970.description_en_long: A mountain of northern Hyogo rising behind the hot springs of Kinosaki Onsen. Its name is said to be read differently from place to place. Fourteen stone Buddhas line its first-order triangulation summit, and the morning sea of clouds here is praised as otherworldly.
970.description_en_short: A peak overlooking Kinosaki Onsen, where stone Buddhas line a summit famed for its sea of clouds.

971.description_ja_long: 能登と越中の境に横たわる標高564mの山。北陸の山岳信仰の一大霊場で、最盛期には院坊360余、衆徒三千を数えたと伝わる。山頂一帯は国の史跡となっている。
