971.description_ja_short: 能登の信仰の山。院坊360余を誇った霊場跡は国の史跡。
971.description_en_long: A 564 m mountain lying on the border of Noto and Etchu. A great center of mountain worship in Hokuriku, it is said to have held over 360 temple lodgings and three thousand monks at its height. The area around the summit is designated a national historic site.
971.description_en_short: A sacred mountain of Noto whose site, once home to over 360 temple lodgings, is a national historic site.

973.description_ja_long: 対馬・厳原の西にそびえる標高558mの山。万葉集の防人歌に詠まれた「対馬の嶺」と伝わり、名は国府から有明の月が懸かる姿を望んだことに由来するという。
973.description_ja_short: 万葉集に詠まれた対馬の山。有明の月に名を負う草原の頂。
973.description_en_long: A 558 m mountain rising west of Izuhara on the island of Tsushima. Held to be the peak of Tsushima sung in the Manyoshu's frontier-guard poems, it is said to be named for the view from the old provincial capital of the dawn moon hanging above it.
973.description_en_short: A Tsushima mountain sung in the Manyoshu, its grassy summit named for the dawn moon.

974.description_ja_long: 伊勢神宮の背後に立つ志摩半島の最高峰。頂に弘法大師開創と伝わる金剛証寺を擁し、死者の魂が宿る「タケ」として岳参りの風習が受け継がれてきた霊山である。
974.description_ja_short: 伊勢神宮の背後に立つ志摩半島の最高峰。岳参りの霊山。
974.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Shima Peninsula, standing behind Ise Jingu shrine. Its summit bears Kongoshoji Temple, said to be founded by Kobo Daishi, and it has long been revered as a sacred 'take' where souls of the dead dwell, visited in the takemairi pilgrimage.
974.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Shima Peninsula behind Ise Jingu, a sacred mountain of the takemairi pilgrimage.

975.description_ja_long: 大阪府能勢町にあり、男山と女山からなる双耳峰。古代、若い男女が集って即興の歌を掛け合った歌垣の舞台と伝わり、筑波山などと並ぶ三大歌垣山に数えられる。
975.description_ja_short: 男女が歌を交わした歌垣の伝承地。男山と女山の双耳峰。
975.description_en_long: A twin-peaked mountain in Nose, Osaka, formed of the male and female summits Otokoyama and Onnayama. Said to be a stage of utagaki, rites where young men and women traded improvised songs, it is counted with Tsukubasan among the three great utagaki mountains.
975.description_en_short: A twin-peaked mountain of Otokoyama and Onnayama, remembered as an ancient stage for utagaki song rites.

977.description_ja_long: 岡山県東部、兵庫県境近くの山。麓の八塔寺村は茅葺き屋根の残る山村風景で知られ、映画「黒い雨」の舞台にも選ばれた。奈良時代創建と伝わる八塔寺を麓に抱く。
977.description_ja_short: 茅葺きの里・八塔寺村と古刹を麓に抱く岡山県境の山。
977.description_en_long: A mountain in eastern Okayama near the Hyogo border. The village of Hattoji at its foot, known for its thatched-roof hamlet scenery, was a setting for the film Black Rain, and Hattoji Temple, said to be founded in the Nara period, also stands at its base.
977.description_en_short: An Okayama border mountain with the thatched-roof village of Hattoji and an ancient temple at its foot.

978.description_ja_long: 出雲平野の北に連なる出雲北山の主峰で、東西65kmに及ぶ島根半島の最高峰。狭い頂は登り着いた実感にあふれ、展望もよく、県内では貴重な登りごたえを誇る。
978.description_ja_short: 島根半島の最高峰。狭い頂に立てば展望が大きく開ける。
978.description_en_long: The main peak of the Izumo Kitayama range north of the Izumo Plain, and the highest point of the Shimane Peninsula, which runs 65 km east to west. Its narrow summit gives a real sense of arrival, opens fine views, and offers a rare rewarding climb within the prefecture.
978.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Shimane Peninsula, whose narrow summit opens onto wide views.

979.description_ja_long: 日本三景・安芸の宮島の最高峰。弘法大師の修行以来千年余り燃え続けるという「不滅の霊火」を守り、原生林に包まれた世界文化遺産の島の頂に立つ。標高535m。
979.description_ja_short: 世界遺産・宮島の最高峰。不滅の霊火が燃え続ける霊山。
979.description_en_long: The highest peak of Miyajima, one of Japan's three most scenic views. At 535 m it crowns this World Heritage island wrapped in primeval forest, guarding the 'eternal sacred flame' said to have burned for over a thousand years since Kobo Daishi's ascetic training.
979.description_en_short: The highest peak of World Heritage Miyajima, a sacred mountain where an eternal flame still burns.

980.description_ja_long: 山口県北東端、須佐にある修験の山。天然記念物の磁石石や中国の黄帝を祀る黄帝社など見所が多く、一等三角点の頂からは日本海の荒波までも一望できる。
980.description_ja_short: 磁石石で知られる須佐の修験の山。日本海を一望する。
980.description_en_long: A Shugendo mountain in Susa, in northeastern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Sights include magnetic rocks designated a natural monument and the Kotei-sha shrine to China's Yellow Emperor, and its first-order triangulation summit surveys even the rough waves of the Sea of Japan.
980.description_en_short: A Shugendo mountain in Susa known for its magnetic rocks, with a summit view over the Sea of Japan.

981.description_ja_long: 札幌市街の南西にそびえる市のシンボル。アイヌ語で「いつも物見をする所」と呼ばれた眺望の山で、山腹には開拓期から守られた森が天然記念物として残る。
981.description_ja_short: 札幌市街を見下ろすシンボル。山腹の原生林は天然記念物。
981.description_en_long: Rising southwest of central Sapporo, this mountain is a symbol of the city. Its Ainu name means 'the place where one always keeps watch,' a nod to its outlook, and the forest on its slopes, protected since the pioneering era, survives as a designated natural monument.
981.description_en_short: Sapporo's symbolic lookout mountain, its slopes cloaked in a protected forest that is a natural monument.

982.description_ja_long: 石垣島の中央にそびえる沖縄県の最高峰で標高526m。名は「島のおおもとをなす山」の意という。イタジイの照葉樹林に包まれ、頂はリュウキュウチクに覆われる。
982.description_ja_short: 石垣島にそびえる沖縄県最高峰。名は「大本の山」の意。
982.description_en_long: Okinawa's highest peak at 526 m, rising at the center of Ishigaki Island. The name is said to mean 'the mountain that forms the island's foundation.' Its slopes are wrapped in evergreen forests of Itajii chinquapin, while the summit is covered with Ryukyu dwarf bamboo.
982.description_en_short: Okinawa's highest peak, rising on Ishigaki Island; its name is said to mean 'the island's foundation.'

983.description_ja_long: 瀬戸内海に突き出た室津半島の最高峰。かつての火山で多孔質の石が転がり、山頂付近には源平合戦ゆかりの史跡が残る古戦場でもある。周囲随一の展望を誇る。
983.description_ja_short: 室津半島の最高峰。源平合戦の史跡が残る古戦場の展望峰。
983.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Murotsu Peninsula, which juts into the Seto Inland Sea. A former volcano scattered with porous stones, it is also an old battlefield where historic sites linked to the Genpei War remain near the summit, and it boasts the finest views in the area.
983.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Murotsu Peninsula, an old Genpei War battlefield with commanding views.

984.description_ja_long: 天草下島のほぼ中央に位置する山。江戸時代には「福連木官山」と呼ばれ、カシの木が将軍家の槍の柄に用いられた。一等三角点の頂から東シナ海まで見渡せる。
984.description_ja_short: 天草下島中央の山。将軍家の槍の柄を生んだカシの森。
984.description_en_long: A mountain near the center of Amakusa Shimoshima island. In the Edo period it was called the Fukuregi official forest, its oak used for the spear shafts of the shogun's family. From its summit with a first-order triangulation point, the view reaches the East China Sea.
984.description_en_short: A mountain at the heart of Amakusa Shimoshima, whose oaks once supplied spear shafts for the shogun's family.

985.description_ja_long: 兵庫県の淡路島にある標高522mの山。山頂に立てば瀬戸内海の展望が広がり、海に囲まれた島の山ならではの大らかな眺めをゆったりと味わうことができる。
985.description_ja_short: 淡路島の山。山頂からは瀬戸内海の大きな展望が広がる。
985.description_en_long: A 522 m mountain on Awaji Island in Hyogo Prefecture. From the summit a view of the Seto Inland Sea spreads wide, and visitors can take their time savoring the open, generous scenery that only a mountain on a sea-girt island can offer.
985.description_en_short: A mountain on Awaji Island whose summit commands a broad view over the Seto Inland Sea.

986.description_ja_long: 茨城県笠間市と石岡市の境に立つ標高518mの山。どの方角から仰いでも端正な円錐形を描く。頂に田上神社が鎮座し、北直下のカタクリ群生地でも名高い。
986.description_ja_short: 端正な円錐形の山。カタクリの群生と山頂の展望で名高い。
986.description_en_long: A 518 m mountain on the border of Kasama and Ishioka in Ibaraki Prefecture, tracing a neat conical form from every direction. Tagami Shrine sits on its summit, and the mountain is also famed for a colony of katakuri dogtooth violets just below the peak to the north.
986.description_en_short: A neatly conical mountain famed for its katakuri flower colony and the view from its summit.

988.description_ja_long: 奈良と大阪の境に雄岳と雌岳を並べる双耳峰。非業の死を遂げた大津皇子を悼み、姉の大伯皇女が詠んだ万葉歌が山名の由来と伝わる。サヌカイトの産地でも知られる。
988.description_ja_short: 雄岳雌岳を並べる双耳峰。大津皇子の哀史を秘めた万葉の山。
988.description_en_long: A twin-peaked mountain on the Nara-Osaka border, pairing the male peak Odake with the female peak Medake. Its name is tied to a Man'yoshu poem by Princess Oku mourning her brother Prince Otsu, who met a tragic death. It is also known as a source of sanukite stone.
988.description_en_short: A twin-peaked mountain of Odake and Medake, a Man'yoshu peak holding the tragic tale of Prince Otsu.

989.description_ja_long: 薩摩の地に東西へ連なる冠岳三峰の最高点。秦の徐福が不老不死の薬を求めて渡来し、この山に冠を捧げたという伝説が名の由来と伝わる。中世は霊場として栄えた。
989.description_ja_short: 徐福伝説に名を負う冠岳の主峰。霊場として栄えた山。
989.description_en_long: The highest of the three Kanmuridake peaks running east to west in old Satsuma. Legend says Xu Fu of Qin, who crossed the sea seeking the elixir of immortality, offered his crown here, giving the mountain its name. In medieval times it flourished as a sacred site.
989.description_en_short: The main peak of Kanmuridake, named for the legend of Xu Fu and once a flourishing sacred site.

990.description_ja_long: 佐賀県武雄市と有田町の境に立つ岩峰で、妙義山を思わせる険しい山容を見せる。源為朝の大蛇退治や夫婦岩の悲恋の伝説が語り継がれ、有田焼の陶石も産する。
990.description_ja_short: 妙義山を思わせる岩峰。大蛇退治の伝説が残る有田の山。
990.description_en_long: A rocky peak on the Takeo-Arita border in Saga Prefecture, with a rugged profile recalling Mt. Myogi. Legends of Minamoto no Tametomo slaying a giant serpent and of tragic lovers at the Meoto-iwa rocks live on here, and it also yields pottery stone for Arita ware.
990.description_en_short: A rocky peak recalling Mt. Myogi, where a legend of a serpent-slaying lives on near Arita.

991.description_ja_long: 屈斜路カルデラの中に盛り上がる活火山。アイヌ語で「裸の山」を意味し、硫黄の気孔が点在する岩山はかつて採掘で賑わった。初夏はエゾイソツツジが荒地を彩る。
991.description_ja_short: アイヌ語で「裸の山」。硫黄を採掘した屈斜路の活火山。
991.description_en_long: An active volcano swelling up inside the Kussharo caldera. Its Ainu name means 'bare mountain,' and the rocky slopes dotted with sulfur vents once bustled with mining. In early summer, Ezo Labrador tea blossoms brighten the barren ground.
991.description_en_short: An active volcano in the Kussharo caldera whose Ainu name means 'bare mountain'; sulfur was once mined here.

992.description_ja_long: マサカリに例えられる下北半島の、柄にあたる部分にそびえる山。陸奥湾と太平洋に挟まれて風は強く、かつて放牧で荒れた山肌が残る。初夏はヤマツツジが彩る。
992.description_ja_short: 下北半島の柄に立つ山。海風吹く頂をヤマツツジが彩る。
992.description_en_long: A mountain rising where the axe-shaped Shimokita Peninsula forms its handle. Squeezed between Mutsu Bay and the Pacific, it is swept by strong winds, and slopes once laid bare by grazing still remain. In early summer, yamatsutsuji azaleas color the mountain.
992.description_en_short: A peak on the handle of the Shimokita Peninsula, its sea-wind-swept summit colored by azaleas.

994.description_ja_long: 北海道寿都町と島牧村にまたがる月越山脈の最高峰。アイヌ語で「大きい盃」を意味する名のとおり、盃を伏せたようななだらかな姿。山裾に天然のブナ林が広がる。
994.description_ja_short: 盃を伏せたような姿の月越山脈最高峰。裾にブナ林が広がる。
994.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Tsukikoshi Range straddling the towns of Suttsu and Shimamaki in Hokkaido. True to its Ainu name meaning 'large sake cup,' it shows a gentle form like an upturned cup, and natural beech forest spreads across its lower slopes.
994.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Tsukikoshi Range, shaped like an upturned cup, with beech forest at its foot.

995.description_ja_long: やんばるの森にそびえる沖縄本島の最高峰で標高503m。山頂域は国の天然保護区域に指定され、ノグチゲラやヤンバルクイナが息づく世界自然遺産の山である。
995.description_ja_short: 沖縄本島最高峰。ヤンバルクイナが息づく世界遺産の森。
995.description_en_long: The highest peak on Okinawa Island at 503 m, rising in the Yambaru forest. Its summit area is designated a national nature conservation area, and the mountain, home to the Okinawa woodpecker and the Okinawa rail, lies within a World Natural Heritage site.
995.description_en_short: Okinawa Island's highest peak, a World Heritage forest where the Okinawa rail lives.

996.description_ja_long: 有明海に面して裾野を広げる小岱山の最高峰。花崗岩の山体に観音岳などの峰を連ね、山頂手前のおむすび型の巨石には落城にまつわる財宝伝説が語り継がれる。
996.description_ja_short: 小岱山の最高峰。巨石に財宝伝説が残る有明海に臨む山。
996.description_en_long: The highest peak of Shodaisan, whose skirts spread toward the Ariake Sea. Its granite massif links peaks such as Kannondake, and a rice-ball-shaped boulder just below the summit carries a legend of treasure hidden when a castle fell.
996.description_en_short: The highest peak of Shodaisan facing the Ariake Sea, where a boulder holds a legend of hidden treasure.

997.description_ja_long: 岡山県の吉備高原東部に位置する標高499mの山。「かなやま」の名をもち、高原の緩やかな起伏の中に頂を置く、地元に親しまれる里山のひとつである。
997.description_ja_short: 吉備高原東部の山。緩やかな起伏の中に頂を置く里山。
997.description_en_long: A 499 m mountain in the eastern Kibi Highlands of Okayama Prefecture. Bearing the name Kanayama, it sets its summit amid the gentle undulations of the plateau and is one of the familiar satoyama hills cherished by the local community.
997.description_en_short: A mountain in the eastern Kibi Highlands, a well-loved satoyama peak amid gentle plateau country.

998.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県トカラ列島の臥蛇島にそびえる標高497mの山。島は人の住まない無人島で、その頂は登る者もないまま洋上に静かに浮かぶ。
998.description_ja_short: トカラ列島の無人島・臥蛇島の頂。訪う人もない洋上の峰。
998.description_en_long: A 497 m mountain rising on Gajajima in the Tokara Islands of Kagoshima Prefecture. The island is uninhabited, and the summit floats quietly above the open sea, with no climbers ever coming to visit it.
998.description_en_short: The summit of uninhabited Gajajima in the Tokara Islands, a quiet peak above the ocean.

999.description_ja_long: トカラ列島に属し奄美大島の北方に浮かぶ無人島の山。大きな火口を抱く円錐形の成層火山で、頂には一等三角点が置かれる。標高495m。
999.description_ja_short: 奄美の北に浮かぶ無人島の火山。頂に一等三角点が眠る。
999.description_en_long: A mountain on an uninhabited island belonging to the Tokara chain, floating north of Amami Oshima. It is a conical stratovolcano holding a large crater, and a first-order triangulation point stands on its summit. Its elevation is 495 m.
999.description_en_short: A volcano on an uninhabited island north of Amami, with a first-order triangulation point on top.
